part.text:
We need work on mise repository. User asks to implement feature: Some GitHub releases attest each file inside an archive rather than archive blob itself. Make mise accept those safely. Security-sensitive; fallback must fail closed. Add thorough focused tests, preserve existing archive-level verification, commit finished change. We can use ask_user for consequential trust-boundary/scope questions; don't guess toward permissiveness.

Need understand codebase. We need investigate GitHub releases attestation verification code. Likely in src/backend/github.rs or src/github.rs. There is attestation verification for GitHub release assets. Some tools release archives where individual files inside archive are attested rather than archive. Need accept those safely: probably verify each file inside extracted archive against attestation, not archive blob. Need fail closed fallback (if can't verify, fail rather than allow).

Need read code. Let's explore.

todos.content: Explore attestation verification code and tests
Determine scope and ask user about trust-boundary choices
Implement safe archive-internal file attestation support
Add focused tests preserving archive-level verification
Run tests/lint and commit

118: /// GitHub artifact attestations are only served by https://api.github.com. GHE
119: /// Server doesn't implement the attestations endpoint, so any verification
120: /// attempt against a custom api_url will fail. Callers gate on this so users
121: /// don't have to disable `MISE_GITHUB_ATTESTATIONS` globally for GHE tools.
122: fn attestations_supported(api_url: &str) -> bool {
123:     api_url.trim_end_matches('/') == DEFAULT_GITHUB_API_BASE_URL
124: }
125: 
126: /// Status returned from verification attempts
127: enum VerificationStatus {
128:     /// No attestations or provenance found (not an error, tool may not have them)
129:     NoAttestations,
130:     /// An error occurred during verification
131:     Error(String),
132: }
133: 
134: /// Check if an SLSA verification error indicates a format/parsing issue rather than
135: /// an actual verification failure. Some provenance files (e.g., BuildKit raw provenance)
136: /// exist but aren't in a sigstore-verifiable format.
137: ///
138: /// `Sigstore(msg)` covers errors that originated in `sigstore-verify` itself
139: /// (e.g. `missing field 'verificationMaterial'` when the file is a legacy
140: /// cosign v1 bundle that the modern bundle deserializer rejects). Those are
141: /// format mismatches, not signature failures, so we let the caller fall
142: /// back to alternate verification paths.
143: fn is_slsa_format_issue(e: &crate::github::sigstore::AttestationError) -> bool {
144:     match e {
145:         crate::github::sigstore::AttestationError::NoAttestations => true,
146:         crate::github::sigstore::AttestationError::UnsupportedFormat(_) => true,
147:         crate::github::sigstore::AttestationError::Verification(msg)
148:         | crate::github::sigstore::AttestationError::Sigstore(msg) => {
149:             msg.contains("does not contain valid attestations")
150:                 || msg.contains("No certificate found")
151:                 || msg.contains("neither DSSE envelope nor message signature")
152:                 || msg.contains("missing field")
153:                 || msg.contains("not a sigstore or cosign bundle")
154:                 || msg.contains("not a JSON DSSE envelope")
155:         }
156:         _ => false,
157:     }
158: }

186:     async fn security_info(&self) -> Vec<SecurityFeature> {
187:         // Only report security features for GitHub (not GitLab yet)
188:         if self.is_gitlab() || self.is_forgejo() {
189:             return vec![];
190:         }
191: 
192:         let mut features = vec![];
193: 
194:         // Get the latest release to check for security assets

197:         let opts = self.options(&raw_opts);
198:         let api_url = opts.api_url();
199:

201:             .await

203: 
204:         let latest_release = releases.first();

206:         // Check for checksum files in assets

209:                 let name = a.name.to_lowercase();
210:                 name.contains("sha256")
211:                     || name.contains("checksum")
212:                     || name.ends_with(".sha256")
213:                     || name.ends_with(".sha512")
214:             });
215:             if has_checksum {

218:                 });
219:             }
220:         }
221: 
222:         // Check for GitHub artifact Attestations (assets with .sigstore.json or .sigstore extension)
223:         if let Some(release) = latest_release {
224:             let has_attestations = release.assets.iter().any(|a| {
225:                 let name = a.name.to_lowercase();
226:                 name.ends_with(".sigstore.json") || name.ends_with(".sigstore")
227:             });
228:             if has_attestations {
229:                 features.push(SecurityFeature::GithubAttestations {
230:                     signer_workflow: None,
231:                 });
232:             }
233:         }
234: 
235:         // Check for SLSA provenance (intoto.jsonl files)
236:         if let Some(release) = latest_release {
237:             let has_slsa = release.assets.iter().any(|a| {
238:                 let name = a.name.to_lowercase();
239:                 name.contains(".intoto.jsonl")
240:                     || name.contains("provenance")
241:                     || name.ends_with(".attestation")
242:             });
243:             if has_slsa {
244:                 features.push(SecurityFeature::Slsa { level: None });
245:             }
246:         }
247:

255:     async fn _list_remote_versions(&self, config: &Arc<Config>) -> Result<Vec<VersionInfo>> {
256:         let repo = self.ba.tool_name();
257:         let id = self.ba.to_string();
258:         let raw_opts = config.get_tool_opts_with_overrides(&self.ba).await?;
259:         let opts = self.options(&raw_opts);
260:         let api_url = opts.api_url();
261:         let version_prefix = opts.version_prefix();
262: 
263:         // Derive web URL base from API URL for enterprise support
264:         let web_url_base = if self.is_gitlab() {
265:             if api_url == DEFAULT_GITLAB_API_BASE_URL {
266:                 format!("https://gitlab.com/{}", repo)
267:             } else {
268:                 // Enterprise GitLab - derive web URL from API URL
269:                 let web_url = api_url.replace("/api/v4", "");
270:                 format!("{}/{}", web_url, repo)
271:             }
272:         } else if self.is_forgejo() {
273:             if api_url == DEFAULT_FORGEJO_API_BASE_URL {
274:                 format!("https://codeberg.org/{}", repo)
275:             } else {
276:                 // Enterprise Forgejo - derive web URL from API URL
277:                 let web_url = api_url.replace("/api/v1", "");
278:                 format!("{}/{}", web_url, repo)
279:             }
280:         } else if api_url == DEFAULT_GITHUB_API_BASE_URL {
281:             format!("https://github.com/{}", repo)
282:         } else {
283:             // Enterprise GitHub - derive web URL from API URL
284:             let web_url = api_url.replace("/api/v3", "").replace("api.", "");
285:             format!("{}/{}", web_url, repo)
286:         };
287: 
288:         // Get releases with full metadata from GitHub, GitLab, or Forgejo
289:         let raw_versions: Vec<VersionInfo> = if self.is_gitlab() {
290:             gitlab::list_releases_from_url(api_url.as_str(), &repo)
291:                 .await?
292:                 .into_iter()
293:                 .filter(|r| version_prefix.is_none_or(|p| r.tag_name.starts_with(p)))
294:                 .map(|r| VersionInfo {
295:                     version: self.strip_version_prefix(&r.tag_name, &opts),
296:                     created_at: r.released_at,
297:                     release_url: Some(format!("{}/-/releases/{}", web_url_base, r.tag_name)),
298:                     ..Default::default()
299:                 })
300:                 .collect()
301:         } else if self.is_forgejo() {
302:             forgejo::list_releases_including_prereleases_from_url(api_url.as_str(), &repo)
303:                 .await?
304:                 .into_iter()
305:                 .filter(|r| version_prefix.is_none_or(|p| r.tag_name.starts_with(p)))
306:                 .map(|r| VersionInfo {
307:                     version: self.strip_version_prefix(&r.tag_name, &opts),
308:                     created_at: Some(r.created_at),
309:                     release_url: Some(format!("{}/releases/tag/{}", web_url_base, r.tag_name)),
310:                     prerelease: r.prerelease,
311:                     ..Default::default()
312:                 })
313:                 .collect()
314:         } else {
315:             // Always fetch the pre-release superset and stamp `prerelease` on
316:             // each entry. The shared remote-versions cache stores the superset
317:             // so flipping the `prerelease` tool option (e.g. via a project
318:             // override) is correct without invalidating the cache; the read
319:             // path filters on `prerelease` according to the current opts.
320:             github::list_releases_including_prereleases_from_url(api_url.as_str(), &repo)
321:                 .await?
322:                 .into_iter()
323:                 .filter(|r| version_prefix.is_none_or(|p| r.tag_name.starts_with(p)))
324:                 .map(|r| VersionInfo {
325:                     version: self.strip_version_prefix(&r.tag_name, &opts),
326:                     created_at: Some(r.created_at),
327:                     release_url: Some(format!("{}/releases/tag/{}", web_url_base, r.tag_name)),
328:                     prerelease: r.prerelease,
329:                     ..Default::default()
330:                 })
331:                 .collect()
332:         };
333: 
334:         // Apply common validation and reverse order
335:         let versions = raw_versions
336:             .into_iter()
337:             .filter(|v| match v.version.parse::<ToolVersionType>() {
338:                 Ok(ToolVersionType::Version(_)) => true,
339:                 _ => {
340:                     warn!("Invalid version: {id}@{}", v.version);
341:                     false
342:                 }
343:             })
344:             .rev()
345:             .collect();
346: 
347:         Ok(versions)
348:     }

350:     async fn latest_stable_version(&self, config: &Arc<Config>) -> eyre::Result<Option<String>> {
351:         if Settings::get().offline() {
352:             trace!("Skipping latest stable version due to offline mode");
353:             return Ok(None);
354:         }
355: 
356:         let repo = self.ba.tool_name();
357:         let raw_opts = config.get_tool_opts_with_overrides(&self.ba).await?;
358:         let opts = self.options(&raw_opts);
359:         let api_url = opts.api_url();
360:         let version_prefix = opts.version_prefix();
361: 
362:         // When `prerelease = true`, skip the `/releases/latest` shortcut
363:         // (which returns whichever release the repo owner marked as "Latest",
364:         // defaulting to the newest non-prerelease). Returning `None` lets the
365:         // trait's `latest_version` fall through to `latest_version_for_query`,
366:         // which resolves against the full list — now including pre-releases.
367:         if self.include_prereleases(opts.raw()) {
368:             return Ok(None);
369:         }
370: 
371:         let latest_tag = if self.is_gitlab() {
372:             // GitLab doesn't have a "latest" endpoint
373:             return Ok(None);
374:         } else if self.is_forgejo() {
375:             match forgejo::get_release_for_url(&api_url, &repo, "latest").await {
376:                 Ok(r) => Some(r.tag_name),
377:                 Err(e) => {
378:                     debug!("Failed to fetch latest Forgejo release for {repo}: {e}");
379:                     None
380:                 }
381:             }
382:         } else {
383:             match github::get_release_for_url(&api_url, &repo, "latest").await {
384:                 Ok(r) => Some(r.tag_name),
385:                 Err(e) => {
386:                     debug!("Failed to fetch latest GitHub release for {repo}: {e}");
387:                     None
388:                 }
389:             }
390:         };
391: 
392:         let latest_version = latest_tag
393:             .filter(|tag| version_prefix.is_none_or(|p| tag.starts_with(p)))
394:             .map(|tag| self.strip_version_prefix(&tag, &opts));
395: 
396:         match latest_version {
397:             Some(version) => Ok(Some(version)),
398:             None => Ok(None),
399:         }
400:     }
401: 
402:     async fn install_version_(
403:         &self,
404:         ctx: &InstallContext,
405:         mut tv: ToolVersion,
406:     ) -> Result<ToolVersion> {
407:         let repo = self.repo();
408:         let raw_opts = ctx.config.get_tool_opts_with_overrides(&self.ba).await?;
409:         let opts = self.options(&raw_opts);
410:         let api_url = opts.api_url();
411: 
412:         // Check if URL already exists in lockfile platforms first
413:         let platform_key = self.get_platform_key();
414: 
415:         let asset = if let Some(existing_platform) = tv.lock_platforms.get(&platform_key)
416:             && existing_platform.url.is_some()
417:         {
418:             debug!(
419:                 "Using existing URL from lockfile for platform {}: {}",
420:                 platform_key,
421:                 existing_platform.url.clone().unwrap_or_default()
422:             );
423:             ReleaseAsset {
424:                 name: get_filename_from_url(existing_platform.url.as_deref().unwrap_or("")),
425:                 url: existing_platform.url.clone().unwrap_or_default(),
426:                 url_api: existing_platform.url_api.clone().unwrap_or_default(),
427:                 digest: None, // Don't use old digest from lockfile, will be fetched fresh if needed
428:             }
429:         } else {
430:             // Find the asset URL for this specific version
431:             self.resolve_asset_url(&tv, &opts, &repo, &api_url).await?
432:         };
433: 
434:         // Download and install
435:         self.download_and_install(ctx, &mut tv, &asset, &opts)
436:             .await?;
437: 
438:         Ok(tv)
439:     }
440: 
441:     async fn list_bin_paths(
442:         &self,
443:         _config: &Arc<Config>,
444:         tv: &ToolVersion,
445:     ) -> Result<Vec<std::path::PathBuf>> {
446:         let raw_opts = tv.request.options();
447:         let opts = self.options(&raw_opts);
448:         let mise_bins_dir = tv.install_path().join(MISE_BINS_DIR);
449:         if opts.filter_bins().is_some() || mise_bins_dir.is_dir() {
450:             return Ok(vec![tv.runtime_path().join(MISE_BINS_DIR)]);
451:         }
452: 
453:         Ok(self
454:             .discover_bin_paths(tv)?
455:             .into_iter()
456:             .map(|path| runtime_path_for_install_path(tv, path))
457:             .collect())
458:     }

460:     fn resolve_lockfile_options(
461:         &self,
462:         request: &ToolRequest,
463:         _target: &PlatformTarget,
464:     ) -> BTreeMap<String, String> {
465:         let raw_opts = request.options();
466:         self.options(&raw_opts).lockfile_options()
467:     }
468: 
469:     /// Resolve platform-specific lock information for cross-platform lockfile generation.
470:     /// This fetches release asset metadata including SHA256 digests from GitHub/GitLab API.
471:     async fn resolve_lock_info(
472:         &self,
473:         tv: &ToolVersion,
474:         target: &PlatformTarget,
475:     ) -> Result<PlatformInfo> {
476:         let repo = self.repo();
477:         let raw_opts = tv.request.options();
478:         let opts = self.options(&raw_opts);
479:         let api_url = opts.api_url();
480: 
481:         // Resolve asset for the target platform
482:         let asset = self
483:             .resolve_asset_url_for_target(tv, &opts, &repo, &api_url, target)
484:             .await;
485: 
486:         match asset {
487:             Ok(asset) => {
488:                 // Detect provenance availability from release assets and attestation API
489:                 let (mut provenance, mut github_attestations) =
490:                     if !self.is_gitlab() && !self.is_forgejo() {
491:                         self.detect_provenance_type(
492:                             tv,
493:                             &opts,
494:                             &repo,
495:                             &api_url,
496:                             asset.digest.as_deref(),
497:                             target,
498:                         )
499:                         .await
500:                     } else {
501:                         (None, None)
502:                     };
503: 
504:                 // For the current platform, verify provenance cryptographically at lock time.
505:                 // This ensures the lockfile's provenance entry is backed by actual verification,
506:                 // not just an API query. Cross-platform entries remain detection-only.
507:                 if provenance.is_some() && target.is_current() {
508:                     match self
509:                         .verify_provenance_at_lock_time(tv, &opts, &repo, &api_url, &asset)
510:                         .await
511:                     {
512:                         Ok((verified, gh_status)) => {
513:                             provenance = verified;
514:                             github_attestations = gh_status;
515:                         }
516:                         Err(e) => {
517:                             // Clear provenance so install-time verification will run.
518:                             warn!(
519:                                 "lock-time provenance verification failed for {}, \
520:                                  will be verified at install time: {e}",
521:                                 self.ba.full()
522:                             );
523:                             provenance = None;
524:                         }
525:                     }
526:                 }
527:                 if provenance.is_some() {
528:                     github_attestations = None;
529:                 }
530: 
531:                 Ok(PlatformInfo {
532:                     url: Some(asset.url),
533:                     url_api: Some(asset.url_api),
534:                     checksum: asset.digest,
535:                     provenance,
536:                     github_attestations,
537:                     ..Default::default()
538:                 })
539:             }
540:             Err(e) => {
541:                 debug!(
542:                     "Failed to resolve asset for {} on {}: {}",
543:                     self.ba.full(),
544:                     target.to_key(),
545:                     e
546:                 );
547:                 Ok(PlatformInfo::default())
548:             }
549:         }
550:     }
551: }
552: 
553: impl UnifiedGitBackend {
554:     pub fn from_arg(ba: BackendArg) -> Self {
555:         Self { ba: Arc::new(ba) }
556:     }
557: 
558:     fn options<'a>(&self, raw: &'a ToolVersionOptions) -> GitBackendOptions<'a> {
559:         GitBackendOptions::new(raw, self.default_api_url())
560:     }
561: 
562:     fn default_api_url(&self) -> &'static str {
563:         if self.is_gitlab() {
564:             DEFAULT_GITLAB_API_BASE_URL
565:         } else if self.is_forgejo() {
566:             DEFAULT_FORGEJO_API_BASE_URL
567:         } else {
568:             DEFAULT_GITHUB_API_BASE_URL
569:         }
570:     }
571: 
572:     /// Detect what provenance type is available for a release by checking its assets
573:     /// and querying the GitHub attestation API.
574:     async fn detect_provenance_type(
575:         &self,
576:         tv: &ToolVersion,
577:         opts: &GitBackendOptions<'_>,
578:         repo: &str,
579:         api_url: &str,
580:         asset_digest: Option<&str>,
581:         target: &PlatformTarget,
582:     ) -> (Option<ProvenanceType>, Option<GithubAttestationsStatus>) {
583:         let settings = Settings::get();
584:         let version = &tv.version;
585:         let version_prefix = opts.version_prefix();
586:         let mut github_attestations = None;
587: 
588:         let release =
589:             try_with_v_prefix_and_repo(version, version_prefix, Some(repo), |candidate| {
590:                 let api_url = api_url.to_string();
591:                 let repo = repo.to_string();
592:                 async move { github::get_release_for_url(&api_url, &repo, &candidate).await }
593:             })
594:             .await
595:             .ok();
596:         let Some(release) = release else {
597:             return (None, None);
598:         };
599: 
600:         // Check github-attestations first (higher priority, matching install verification order)
601:         // Uses the asset digest from the GitHub API to query attestations without downloading
602:         if settings.github_attestations
603:             && settings.github.github_attestations
604:             && attestations_supported(api_url)
605:             && let Some(digest) = asset_digest
606:         {
607:             let parts: Vec<&str> = repo.split('/').collect();
608:             if parts.len() == 2 {
609:                 let (owner, repo_name) = (parts[0], parts[1]);
610:                 match crate::github::sigstore::detect_attestations(
611:                     owner, repo_name, api_url, digest,
612:                 )
613:                 .await
614:                 {
615:                     Ok(true) => return (Some(ProvenanceType::GithubAttestations), None),
616:                     Ok(false) => {
617:                         github_attestations = Some(GithubAttestationsStatus::Unavailable);
618:                     }
619:                     Err(crate::github::sigstore::DetectError::SourceCreation(e)) => {
620:                         warn!(
621:                             "Failed to create GitHub attestation source for {owner}/{repo_name}: {e}. \
622:                              Lockfile may not record github-attestations provenance."
623:                         );
624:                     }
625:                     Err(crate::github::sigstore::DetectError::Fetch(e)) => {
626:                         warn!(
627:                             "GitHub attestation API query failed for {owner}/{repo_name}: {e}. \
628:                              Lockfile may not record github-attestations provenance."
629:                         );
630:                     }
631:                 }
632:             }
633:         }
634: 
635:         // Check for SLSA provenance from release assets using the same platform-aware
636:         // picker as install-time verification. This ensures we only record SLSA provenance
637:         // when a matching provenance file exists for the target platform.
638:         if settings.slsa && settings.github.slsa {
639:             let asset_names: Vec<String> = release.assets.iter().map(|a| a.name.clone()).collect();
640:             let picker = AssetPicker::with_libc(
641:                 target.os_name().to_string(),
642:                 target.arch_name().to_string(),
643:                 target.qualifier().map(|s| s.to_string()),
644:             );
645:             if let Some(provenance_name) = picker.pick_best_provenance(&asset_names) {
646:                 let url = release
647:                     .assets
648:                     .iter()
649:                     .find(|a| a.name == provenance_name)
650:                     .map(|a| a.browser_download_url.clone());
651:                 return (Some(ProvenanceType::Slsa { url }), None);
652:             }
653:         }
654: 
655:         (None, github_attestations)
656:     }
657: 
658:     /// Verify provenance at lock time by downloading the artifact to a temp directory
659:     /// and running cryptographic verification. Only called for the current platform
660:     /// during `mise lock`.
661:     async fn verify_provenance_at_lock_time(
662:         &self,
663:         tv: &ToolVersion,
664:         opts: &GitBackendOptions<'_>,
665:         repo: &str,
666:         api_url: &str,
667:         asset: &ReleaseAsset,
668:     ) -> Result<(Option<ProvenanceType>, Option<GithubAttestationsStatus>)> {
669:         let tmp_dir = tempfile::tempdir()?;
670:         let filename = get_filename_from_url(&asset.url);
671:         let artifact_path = tmp_dir.path().join(&filename);
672: 
673:         info!(
674:             "downloading artifact for lock-time provenance verification: {}",
675:             filename
676:         );
677: 
678:         // Use the API URL with appropriate headers for downloading
679:         let download_url = if self.is_gitlab() {
680:             asset.url.clone()
681:         } else {
682:             asset.url_api.clone()
683:         };
684:         let headers = if self.is_gitlab() {
685:             gitlab::get_headers(&download_url)
686:         } else if self.is_forgejo() {
687:             forgejo::get_headers(&download_url)
688:         } else {
689:             github::get_headers(&download_url)
690:         };
691:         HTTP.download_file_with_headers(&download_url, &artifact_path, &headers, None)
692:             .await?;
693: 
694:         let settings = Settings::get();
695:         let mut github_attestations = None;
696: 
697:         // Try GitHub artifact attestations first (highest priority)
698:         if settings.github_attestations
699:             && settings.github.github_attestations
700:             && attestations_supported(api_url)
701:         {
702:             let parts: Vec<&str> = repo.split('/').collect();
703:             if parts.len() == 2 {
704:                 let (owner, repo_name) = (parts[0], parts[1]);
705:                 match crate::github::sigstore::verify_attestation(
706:                     &artifact_path,
707:                     owner,
708:                     repo_name,
709:                     None,
710:                     Some(api_url),
711:                 )
712:                 .await
713:                 {
714:                     Ok(true) => {
715:                         debug!("lock-time GitHub attestations verified for {}", repo);
716:                         return Ok((Some(ProvenanceType::GithubAttestations), None));
717:                     }
718:                     Ok(false) => {
719:                         return Err(eyre::eyre!(
720:                             "GitHub artifact attestations verification returned false"
721:                         ));
722:                     }
723:                     Err(crate::github::sigstore::AttestationError::NoAttestations) => {
724:                         github_attestations = Some(GithubAttestationsStatus::Unavailable);
725:                         debug!("no GitHub attestations found at lock time, trying SLSA");
726:                     }
727:                     Err(e) => {
728:                         return Err(eyre::eyre!(
729:                             "GitHub artifact attestations verification failed: {e}"
730:                         ));
731:                     }
732:                 }
733:             }
734:         }
735: 
736:         // Fall back to SLSA provenance
737:         if settings.slsa && settings.github.slsa {
738:             let version = &tv.version;
739:             let version_prefix = opts.version_prefix();
740:             let release =
741:                 try_with_v_prefix_and_repo(version, version_prefix, Some(repo), |candidate| {
742:                     let api_url = api_url.to_string();
743:                     let repo = repo.to_string();
744:                     async move { github::get_release_for_url(&api_url, &repo, &candidate).await }
745:                 })
746:                 .await?;
747: 
748:             let asset_names: Vec<String> = release.assets.iter().map(|a| a.name.clone()).collect();
749:             let current_platform = PlatformTarget::from_current();
750:             let picker = AssetPicker::with_libc(
751:                 current_platform.os_name().to_string(),
752:                 current_platform.arch_name().to_string(),
753:                 current_platform.qualifier().map(|s| s.to_string()),
754:             );
755: 
756:             if let Some(provenance_name) = picker.pick_best_provenance(&asset_names) {
757:                 let provenance_asset = release
758:                     .assets
759:                     .iter()
760:                     .find(|a| a.name == provenance_name)
761:                     .expect("provenance asset should exist since we found its name");
762: 
763:                 let provenance_path = tmp_dir.path().join(&provenance_asset.name);
764:                 HTTP.download_file(
765:                     &provenance_asset.browser_download_url,
766:                     &provenance_path,
767:                     None,
768:                 )
769:                 .await?;
770: 
771:                 let provenance_url = provenance_asset.browser_download_url.clone();
772:                 match crate::github::sigstore::verify_slsa_provenance(
773:                     &artifact_path,
774:                     &provenance_path,
775:                     1u8,
776:                 )
777:                 .await
778:                 {
779:                     Ok(true) => {
780:                         debug!("lock-time SLSA provenance verified for {}", repo);
781:                         return Ok((
782:                             Some(ProvenanceType::Slsa {
783:                                 url: Some(provenance_url),
784:                             }),
785:                             None,
786:                         ));
787:                     }
788:                     Ok(false) => {
789:                         return Err(eyre::eyre!("SLSA provenance verification failed"));
790:                     }
791:                     Err(e) => {
792:                         if is_slsa_format_issue(&e) {
793:                             debug!("SLSA provenance file not in verifiable format: {e}");
794:                         } else {
795:                             return Err(eyre::eyre!("SLSA verification error: {e}"));
796:                         }
797:                     }
798:                 }
799:             }
800:         }
801: 
802:         if github_attestations.is_some() {
803:             Ok((None, github_attestations))
804:         } else {
805:             Err(eyre::eyre!(
806:                 "provenance was detected but could not be verified at lock time"
807:             ))
808:         }
809:     }
810: 
811:     fn is_gitlab(&self) -> bool {
812:         self.ba.backend_type() == BackendType::Gitlab
813:     }
814: 
815:     fn is_forgejo(&self) -> bool {
816:         self.ba.backend_type() == BackendType::Forgejo
817:     }
818: 
819:     fn repo(&self) -> String {
820:         // Use tool_name() method to properly resolve aliases
821:         // This ensures that when an alias like "test-edit = github:microsoft/edit" is used,
822:         // the repository name is correctly extracted as "microsoft/edit"
823:         self.ba.tool_name()
824:     }
825: 
826:     // Helper to format asset names for error messages
827:     fn format_asset_list<'a, I>(assets: I) -> String
828:     where
829:         I: Iterator<Item = &'a String>,
830:     {
831:         assets.cloned().collect::<Vec<_>>().join(", ")
832:     }
833: 
834:     /// Downloads and installs the asset
835:     async fn download_and_install(
836:         &self,
837:         ctx: &InstallContext,
838:         tv: &mut ToolVersion,
839:         asset: &ReleaseAsset,
840:         opts: &GitBackendOptions<'_>,
841:     ) -> Result<()> {
842:         let filename = asset.name.clone();
843:         let file_path = tv.download_path().join(&filename);
844: 
845:         // Check if we'll verify checksum
846:         let has_checksum = opts.checksum().is_some();
847: 
848:         // Store the asset URL and digest (if available) in the tool version
849:         let platform_key = self.get_platform_key();
850:         let platform_info = tv.lock_platforms.entry(platform_key).or_default();
851:         platform_info.url = Some(asset.url.clone());
852:         platform_info.url_api = Some(asset.url_api.clone());
853:         if let Some(digest) = &asset.digest {
854:             debug!("using GitHub API digest for checksum verification");
855:             platform_info.checksum = Some(digest.clone());
856:         }
857: 
858:         let url = match asset.url_api.starts_with(DEFAULT_GITHUB_API_BASE_URL)
859:             || asset.url_api.starts_with(DEFAULT_GITLAB_API_BASE_URL)
860:             || asset.url_api.starts_with(DEFAULT_FORGEJO_API_BASE_URL)
861:         {
862:             // check if url is reachable, 404 might indicate a private repo or asset.
863:             // This is needed, because private repos and assets cannot be downloaded
864:             // via browser url, therefore a fallback to api_url is needed in such cases.
865:             // Also check Content-Type - if it's text/html, we got a login page (private repo).
866:             true => match HTTP.head(asset.url.clone()).await {
867:                 Ok(resp) => {
868:                     let content_type = resp
869:                         .headers()
870:                         .get("content-type")
871:                         .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())
872:                         .unwrap_or("");
873:                     if content_type.contains("text/html") {
874:                         debug!("Browser URL returned HTML (likely auth page), using API URL");
875:                         asset.url_api.clone()
876:                     } else {
877:                         asset.url.clone()
878:                     }
879:                 }
880:                 Err(_) => asset.url_api.clone(),
881:             },
882: 
883:             // Custom API URLs usually imply that a custom GitHub/GitLab instance is used.
884:             // Often times such instances do not allow browser URL downloads, e.g. due to
885:             // upstream company SSOs. Therefore, using the api_url for downloading is the safer approach.
886:             false => {
887:                 debug!(
888:                     "Since the tool resides on a custom GitHub/GitLab API ({:?}), the asset download will be performed using the given API instead of browser URL download",
889:                     asset.url_api
890:                 );
891:                 asset.url_api.clone()
892:             }
893:         };
894: 
895:         let headers = if self.is_gitlab() {
896:             gitlab::get_headers(&url)
897:         } else if self.is_forgejo() {
898:             forgejo::get_headers(&url)
899:         } else {
900:             github::get_headers(&url)
901:         };
902: 
903:         ctx.pr.set_message(format!("download {filename}"));
904:         HTTP.download_file_with_headers(url, &file_path, &headers, Some(ctx.pr.as_ref()))
905:             .await?;
906: 
907:         // Verify and install
908:         ctx.pr.next_operation();
909:         if has_checksum {
910:             verify_artifact(tv, &file_path, opts.raw(), Some(ctx.pr.as_ref()))?;
911:         }
912: 
913:         // Check before verify_checksum, which may generate a new checksum from the
914:         // downloaded file. We only want to skip provenance when the lockfile already
915:         // had integrity data before this install.
916:         let platform_key = self.get_platform_key();
917:         let has_lockfile_integrity = tv
918:             .lock_platforms
919:             .get(&platform_key)
920:             .is_some_and(PlatformInfo::has_checksum_and_verified_provenance);
921: 
922:         self.verify_checksum(ctx, tv, &file_path)?;
923: 
924:         let settings = Settings::get();
925:         let force_verify = settings.force_provenance_verify();
926:         if has_lockfile_integrity && !force_verify {
927:             // Still check that the recorded provenance type's setting is enabled —
928:             // disabling a verification setting with a provenance-bearing lockfile is a downgrade.
929:             self.ensure_provenance_setting_enabled(tv, &platform_key)?;
930:         } else {
931:             let (provenance_result, github_attestations) = self
932:                 .verify_attestations_or_slsa(ctx, tv, &file_path)
933:                 .await?;
934: 
935:             // Record provenance verification result in lock_platforms
936:             if provenance_result.is_some() || github_attestations.is_some() {
937:                 let platform_info = tv.lock_platforms.entry(platform_key).or_default();
938:                 platform_info.provenance = provenance_result;
939:                 platform_info.github_attestations = github_attestations;
940:             }
941:         }
942: 
943:         ctx.pr.next_operation();
944:         install_artifact(tv, &file_path, opts.raw(), Some(ctx.pr.as_ref()))?;
945: 
946:         if let Some(bins) = opts.filter_bins() {
947:             self.create_symlink_bin_dir(tv, bins)?;
948:         }
949: 
950:         Ok(())
951:     }
952: 
953:     /// Discovers bin paths in the installation directory
954:     fn discover_bin_paths(&self, tv: &ToolVersion) -> Result<Vec<std::path::PathBuf>> {
955:         let raw_opts = tv.request.options();
956:         let opts = self.options(&raw_opts);
957:         if let Some(bin_path_template) = opts.bin_path() {
958:             let bin_path = template_string(&bin_path_template, tv);

963:         if bin_path.exists() {
964:             return Ok(vec![bin_path]);
965:         }
966: 
967:         // Check for macOS .app bundle structure at root (happens when auto-strip removed .app wrapper)
968:         // Look for Contents/MacOS/ which indicates a stripped .app bundle
969:         let contents_macos = tv.install_path().join("Contents").join("MacOS");
970:         if contents_macos.is_dir() {

974:         // Check if the root directory contains an executable file

976:         if let Ok(entries) = std::fs::read_dir(tv.install_path()) {
977:             for entry in entries.flatten() {
978:                 let path = entry.path();
979:                 if path.is_file() && file::is_executable(&path) {
980:                     return Ok(vec![tv.install_path()]);

984: 
985:         // Look for bin directory or executables in subdirectories (for extracted archives)
986:         let mut paths = Vec::new();

988:             for entry in entries.flatten() {

991:                     // Check for macOS .app bundles (e.g., SwiftFormat.app/Contents/MacOS/)
992:                     let path_str = path.file_name().unwrap_or_default().to_string_lossy();

1006:                         if let Ok(sub_entries) = std::fs::read_dir(&path) {
1007:                             for sub_entry in sub_entries.flatten() {
1008:                                 let sub_path = sub_entry.path();

1387:     fn strip_version_prefix(&self, tag_name: &str, opts: &GitBackendOptions<'_>) -> String {
1388:         // If a custom version_prefix is configured, strip it first
1389:         if let Some(prefix) = opts.version_prefix()
1390:             && let Some(stripped) = tag_name.strip_prefix(prefix)
1391:         {
1392:             return stripped.to_string();
1393:         }
1394: 
1395:         // Handle projectname@version format (e.g., "tectonic@0.15.0" -> "0.15.0")
1396:         // Only strip if the prefix matches the repo short name or full repo name to ensure
1397:         // we can reconstruct the tag later during installation. For repos with multiple
1398:         // packages (e.g., tectonic@ and tectonic_xetex_layout@), users must configure
1399:         // version_prefix to install packages that don't match the repo name.
1400:         if let Some(caps) = regex!(r"^([^@]+)@(\d.*)$").captures(tag_name) {
1401:             let prefix = caps.get(1).unwrap().as_str();
1402:             let version = caps.get(2).unwrap().as_str();
1403:             let repo = self.repo();
1404:             let repo_short_name = repo.split('/').next_back();
1405:             // Strip if prefix matches repo short name OR full repo name
1406:             if repo_short_name == Some(prefix) || repo == prefix {
1407:                 return version.to_string();
1408:             }
1409:         }
1410: 
1411:         // Fall back to stripping 'v' prefix
1412:         if tag_name.starts_with('v') {
1413:             tag_name.trim_start_matches('v').to_string()
1414:         } else {
1415:             tag_name.to_string()
1416:         }

1519:         &self,
1520:         ctx: &InstallContext,
1521:         tv: &ToolVersion,
1522:         file_path: &std::path::Path,
1523:     ) -> Result<(Option<ProvenanceType>, Option<GithubAttestationsStatus>)> {
1524:         let settings = Settings::get();
1525: 
1526:         // Read the expected provenance from the lockfile. We use .clone() because tv is
1527:         // &ToolVersion. The result is validated against this expectation at every return
1528:         // point: successful verification checks type match, and no-verification triggers
1529:         // a downgrade error.
1530:         let platform_key = self.get_platform_key();
1531:         let locked_provenance = tv
1532:             .lock_platforms
1533:             .get(&platform_key)
1534:             .and_then(|pi| pi.provenance.clone());
1535:         let expected_provenance = locked_provenance.as_ref();
1536:         let skip_cached_absent_attestations = !settings.force_provenance_verify()
1537:             && tv
1538:                 .lock_platforms
1539:                 .get(&platform_key)
1540:                 .is_some_and(PlatformInfo::has_checksum_and_github_attestations_unavailable);
1541:         let mut github_attestations_unavailable = skip_cached_absent_attestations;
1542: 
1543:         // Only verify for GitHub repos (not GitLab/Forgejo)
1544:         if self.is_gitlab() || self.is_forgejo() {
1545:             if let Some(expected) = expected_provenance {
1546:                 return Err(eyre::eyre!(
1547:                     "Lockfile requires {expected} provenance for {tv} but verification is not available \
1548:                      for GitLab/Forgejo backends. This may indicate a downgrade attack."
1549:                 ));
1550:             }
1551:             return Ok((None, None));
1552:         }
1553: 
1554:         // When the lockfile specifies a provenance type, only run that specific mechanism
1555:         let skip_attestations = skip_cached_absent_attestations
1556:             || expected_provenance.is_some_and(|l| !l.is_github_attestations());
1557:         let skip_slsa = expected_provenance.is_some_and(|l| !l.is_slsa());
1558: 
1559:         // If the lockfile expects github-attestations but the configured api_url
1560:         // doesn't support them (e.g. GHE Server), surface a clear, actionable
1561:         // error rather than falling through to the generic "downgrade attack"
1562:         // path below.
1563:         let raw_opts = tv.request.options();
1564:         let opts = self.options(&raw_opts);
1565:         let api_url = opts.api_url();
1566:         if !attestations_supported(&api_url)
1567:             && let Some(expected) = expected_provenance
1568:             && expected.is_github_attestations()
1569:         {
1570:             return Err(eyre::eyre!(
1571:                 "Lockfile requires github-attestations provenance for {tv} but the \
1572:                  configured api_url ({api_url}) does not serve attestations. \
1573:                  Re-run `mise lock` to refresh the lockfile, or remove the custom api_url."
1574:             ));
1575:         }
1576: 
1577:         // Try GitHub artifact attestations first (if enabled globally and for github backend)
1578:         if !skip_attestations
1579:             && settings.github_attestations
1580:             && settings.github.github_attestations
1581:             && attestations_supported(&api_url)
1582:         {
1583:             match self
1584:                 .try_verify_github_attestations(ctx, tv, file_path, &api_url)
1585:                 .await
1586:             {
1587:                 Ok(true) => {
1588:                     // Defense-in-depth: verify the result matches the lockfile expectation
1589:                     if let Some(expected) = expected_provenance
1590:                         && !expected.is_github_attestations()
1591:                     {
1592:                         return Err(eyre::eyre!(
1593:                             "Lockfile requires {expected} provenance for {tv} but github-attestations was verified. \
1594:                              This may indicate a provenance type mismatch."
1595:                         ));
1596:                     }
1597:                     return Ok((Some(ProvenanceType::GithubAttestations), None));
1598:                 }
1599:                 Ok(false) => {
1600:                     // Attestations exist but verification failed - hard error
1601:                     return Err(eyre::eyre!(
1602:                         "GitHub artifact attestations verification failed for {tv}"
1603:                     ));
1604:                 }
1605:                 Err(VerificationStatus::NoAttestations) => {
1606:                     // No attestations - fall through to try SLSA
1607:                     github_attestations_unavailable = true;
1608:                     debug!("No GitHub artifact attestations found for {tv}, trying SLSA");
1609:                 }
1610:                 Err(VerificationStatus::Error(e)) => {
1611:                     // Error during verification - hard error
1612:                     return Err(eyre::eyre!(
1613:                         "GitHub artifact attestations verification error for {tv}: {e}"
1614:                     ));
1615:                 }
1616:             }
1617:         }
1618: 
1619:         // Fall back to SLSA provenance (if enabled globally and for github backend)
1620:         if !skip_slsa && settings.slsa && settings.github.slsa {
1621:             match self.try_verify_slsa(ctx, tv, file_path, &api_url).await {
1622:                 Ok((true, provenance_url)) => {
1623:                     // Defense-in-depth: verify the result matches the lockfile expectation
1624:                     if let Some(expected) = expected_provenance
1625:                         && !expected.is_slsa()
1626:                     {
1627:                         return Err(eyre::eyre!(
1628:                             "Lockfile requires {expected} provenance for {tv} but slsa was verified. \
1629:                              This may indicate a provenance type mismatch."
1630:                         ));
1631:                     }
1632:                     return Ok((
1633:                         Some(ProvenanceType::Slsa {
1634:                             url: provenance_url,
1635:                         }),
1636:                         None,
1637:                     ));
1638:                 }
1639:                 Ok((false, _)) => {
1640:                     // Provenance exists but verification failed - hard error
1641:                     return Err(eyre::eyre!("SLSA provenance verification failed for {tv}"));
1642:                 }
1643:                 Err(VerificationStatus::NoAttestations) => {
1644:                     // No provenance found - this is fine
1645:                     debug!("No SLSA provenance found for {tv}");
1646:                 }
1647:                 Err(VerificationStatus::Error(e)) => {
1648:                     // Error during verification - hard error
1649:                     return Err(eyre::eyre!("SLSA verification error for {tv}: {e}"));
1650:                 }
1651:             }
1652:         }
1653: 
1654:         // If lockfile recorded provenance but no verification succeeded, it's a downgrade attack
1655:         if let Some(expected) = expected_provenance {
1656:             return Err(eyre::eyre!(
1657:                 "Lockfile requires {expected} provenance for {tv} but verification was not performed. \
1658:                  This may indicate a downgrade attack. Enable the corresponding verification setting \
1659:                  or update the lockfile."
1660:             ));
1661:         }
1662: 
1663:         if github_attestations_unavailable
1664:             && tv
1665:                 .lock_platforms
1666:                 .get(&platform_key)
1667:                 .is_some_and(|pi| pi.checksum.is_some())
1668:         {
1669:             Ok((None, Some(GithubAttestationsStatus::Unavailable)))
1670:         } else {
1671:             Ok((None, None))
1672:         }
1673:     }
1674: 
1675:     /// Try to verify GitHub artifact attestations. Returns:
1676:     /// - Ok(true) if attestations exist and verified successfully
1677:     /// - Ok(false) if attestations exist but verification failed
1678:     /// - Err(NoAttestations) if no attestations found
1679:     /// - Err(Error) if an error occurred during verification
1680:     async fn try_verify_github_attestations(
1681:         &self,
1682:         ctx: &InstallContext,
1683:         tv: &ToolVersion,
1684:         file_path: &std::path::Path,
1685:         api_url: &str,
1686:     ) -> std::result::Result<bool, VerificationStatus> {
1687:         ctx.pr
1688:             .set_message("verify GitHub artifact attestations".to_string());
1689: 
1690:         // Parse owner/repo from the repo string
1691:         let repo = self.repo();
1692:         let parts: Vec<&str> = repo.split('/').collect();
1693:         if parts.len() != 2 {
1694:             return Err(VerificationStatus::Error(format!(
1695:                 "Invalid repo format: {repo}"
1696:             )));
1697:         }
1698:         let (owner, repo_name) = (parts[0], parts[1]);
1699: 
1700:         match crate::github::sigstore::verify_attestation(
1701:             file_path,
1702:             owner,
1703:             repo_name,
1704:             None, // We don't know the expected workflow
1705:             Some(api_url),
1706:         )
1707:         .await
1708:         {
1709:             Ok(verified) => {
1710:                 if verified {
1711:                     ctx.pr
1712:                         .set_message("✓ GitHub artifact attestations verified".to_string());
1713:                     debug!("GitHub artifact attestations verified successfully for {tv}");
1714:                 }
1715:                 Ok(verified)
1716:             }
1717:             Err(crate::github::sigstore::AttestationError::NoAttestations) => {
1718:                 Err(VerificationStatus::NoAttestations)
1719:             }
1720:             Err(e) => Err(VerificationStatus::Error(e.to_string())),
1721:         }
1722:     }
1723: 
1724:     /// Try to verify SLSA provenance. Returns:
1725:     /// - Ok((true, Some(url))) if provenance exists and verified successfully
1726:     /// - Ok((false, _)) if provenance exists but verification failed
1727:     /// - Err(NoAttestations) if no provenance found
1728:     /// - Err(Error) if an error occurred during verification
1729:     async fn try_verify_slsa(
1730:         &self,
1731:         ctx: &InstallContext,
1732:         tv: &ToolVersion,
1733:         file_path: &std::path::Path,
1734:         api_url: &str,
1735:     ) -> std::result::Result<(bool, Option<String>), VerificationStatus> {
1736:         if self.is_gitlab() || self.is_forgejo() {
1737:             return Err(VerificationStatus::NoAttestations);
1738:         }
1739: 
1740:         ctx.pr.set_message("verify SLSA provenance".to_string());
1741: 
1742:         // Get the release to find provenance assets
1743:         let repo = self.repo();
1744:         let raw_opts = tv.request.options();
1745:         let opts = self.options(&raw_opts);
1746:         let version = &tv.version;
1747: 
1748:         // Try to get the release (with version prefix support)
1749:         let version_prefix = opts.version_prefix();
1750:         let release =
1751:             match try_with_v_prefix_and_repo(version, version_prefix, Some(&repo), |candidate| {
1752:                 let api_url = api_url.to_string();
1753:                 let repo = repo.clone();
1754:                 async move { github::get_release_for_url(&api_url, &repo, &candidate).await }
1755:             })
1756:             .await
1757:             {
1758:                 Ok(r) => r,
1759:                 Err(e) => {
1760:                     return Err(VerificationStatus::Error(format!(
1761:                         "Failed to get release: {e}"
1762:                     )));
1763:                 }
1764:             };
1765: 
1766:         // Find the best provenance asset for the current platform
1767:         let asset_names: Vec<String> = release.assets.iter().map(|a| a.name.clone()).collect();
1768:         let current_platform = PlatformTarget::from_current();
1769:         let picker = AssetPicker::with_libc(
1770:             current_platform.os_name().to_string(),
1771:             current_platform.arch_name().to_string(),
1772:             current_platform.qualifier().map(|s| s.to_string()),
1773:         );
1774: 
1775:         let provenance_name = match picker.pick_best_provenance(&asset_names) {
1776:             Some(name) => name,
1777:             None => return Err(VerificationStatus::NoAttestations),
1778:         };
1779: 
1780:         let provenance_asset = release
1781:             .assets
1782:             .iter()
1783:             .find(|a| a.name == provenance_name)
1784:             .expect("provenance asset should exist since we found its name");
1785: 
1786:         // Download the provenance file
1787:         let download_dir = tv.download_path();
1788:         let provenance_path = download_dir.join(&provenance_asset.name);
1789: 
1790:         ctx.pr
1791:             .set_message(format!("download {}", provenance_asset.name));
1792:         if let Err(e) = HTTP
1793:             .download_file(
1794:                 &provenance_asset.browser_download_url,
1795:                 &provenance_path,
1796:                 Some(ctx.pr.as_ref()),
1797:             )
1798:             .await
1799:         {
1800:             return Err(VerificationStatus::Error(format!(
1801:                 "Failed to download provenance: {e}"
1802:             )));
1803:         }
1804: 
1805:         ctx.pr.set_message("verify SLSA provenance".to_string());
1806: 
1807:         // Verify the provenance
1808:         let provenance_download_url = provenance_asset.browser_download_url.clone();
1809:         match crate::github::sigstore::verify_slsa_provenance(
1810:             file_path,
1811:             &provenance_path,
1812:             1, // Minimum SLSA level
1813:         )
1814:         .await
1815:         {
1816:             Ok(verified) => {
1817:                 if verified {
1818:                     debug!("SLSA provenance verified successfully for {tv}");
1819:                     Ok((true, Some(provenance_download_url)))
1820:                 } else {
1821:                     Ok((false, None))
1822:                 }
1823:             }
1824:             Err(e) => {
1825:                 if is_slsa_format_issue(&e) {
1826:                     debug!("SLSA provenance file not in verifiable format for {tv}: {e}");
1827:                     Err(VerificationStatus::NoAttestations)
1828:                 } else {
1829:                     Err(VerificationStatus::Error(e.to_string()))
1830:                 }
1831:             }
1832:         }
1833:     }
1834: }
1835: 
1836: /// Templates a string pattern with version and target platform values
1837: fn template_string_for_target(template: &str, tv: &ToolVersion, target: &PlatformTarget) -> String {
1838:     let version = &tv.version;
1839:     let os = target.os_name();
1840:     let arch = target.arch_name();
1841: 
1842:     // Map to common naming conventions
1843:     let darwin_os = if os == "macos" { "darwin" } else { os };
1844:     let amd64_arch = match arch {
1845:         "x64" => "amd64",
1846:         _ => arch, // arm64 stays as "arm64" in amd64/arm64 convention
1847:     };
1848:     let x86_64_arch = match arch {
1849:         "x64" => "x86_64",
1850:         "arm64" => "aarch64",
1851:         _ => arch,
1852:     };
1853:     // GNU-style arch: x64 -> x86_64, arm64 stays arm64 (used by opam, etc.)
1854:     let gnu_arch = match arch {
1855:         "x64" => "x86_64",
1856:         _ => arch,
1857:     };
1858: 
1859:     // Check for legacy {placeholder} syntax (any of the supported placeholders)
1860:     let has_legacy_placeholder = [
1861:         "{version}",
1862:         "{os}",
1863:         "{arch}",
1864:         "{darwin_os}",
1865:         "{amd64_arch}",
1866:         "{x86_64_arch}",
1867:         "{gnu_arch}",
1868:     ]
1869:     .iter()
1870:     .any(|p| template.contains(p) && !template.contains(&format!("{{{p}}}")));
1871: 
1872:     if has_legacy_placeholder {
1873:         deprecated_at!(
1874:             "2026.3.0",
1875:             "2027.3.0",
1876:             "legacy-version-template",
1877:             "Use Tera syntax (e.g., {{{{ version }}}}) instead of legacy {{version}} in templates"
1878:         );
1879:         // Legacy support: replace {placeholder} patterns
1880:         return template
1881:             .replace("{version}", version)
1882:             .replace("{os}", os)
1883:             .replace("{arch}", arch)
1884:             .replace("{darwin_os}", darwin_os)
1885:             .replace("{amd64_arch}", amd64_arch)
1886:             .replace("{x86_64_arch}", x86_64_arch)
1887:             .replace("{gnu_arch}", gnu_arch);
1888:     }
1889: 
1890:     if !crate::tera::contains_template_syntax(template) {
1891:         return template.to_string();
1892:     }
1893: 
1894:     // Use Tera rendering for templates
1895:     let mut ctx = crate::tera::BASE_CONTEXT.clone();
1896:     ctx.insert("version", version);
1897:     ctx.insert("os", os);
1898:     ctx.insert("arch", arch);
1899:     ctx.insert("darwin_os", darwin_os);
1900:     ctx.insert("amd64_arch", amd64_arch);
1901:     ctx.insert("x86_64_arch", x86_64_arch);
1902:     ctx.insert("gnu_arch", gnu_arch);
1903: 
1904:     let mut tera = crate::tera::get_tera(None);
1905:     // Register target-aware os() and arch() functions that use the target platform
1906:     // instead of the compile-time platform
1907:     let make_remapping_fn = |value: String| {
1908:         move |args: &HashMap<String, tera::Value>| -> tera::Result<tera::Value> {
1909:             if let Some(s) = args.get(value.as_str()).and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {
1910:                 Ok(tera::Value::String(s.to_string()))
1911:             } else {
1912:                 Ok(tera::Value::String(value.clone()))
1913:             }
1914:         }
1915:     };
1916:     tera.register_function("os", make_remapping_fn(os.to_string()));
1917:     tera.register_function("arch", make_remapping_fn(arch.to_string()));
1918: 
1919:     match crate::tera::render_str(&mut tera, template, &ctx) {
1920:         Ok(rendered) => rendered,
1921:         Err(e) => {
1922:             warn!("Failed to render template '{}': {}", template, e);
1923:             template.to_string()
1924:         }
1925:     }
1926: }

1928: #[cfg(test)]
1929: mod tests {
1930:     use super::*;
1931:     use crate::cli::args::BackendArg;
1932: 
1933:     fn create_test_backend() -> UnifiedGitBackend {
1934:         UnifiedGitBackend::from_arg(BackendArg::new(
1935:             "github:test/repo".to_string(),
1936:             Some("github:test/repo".to_string()),
1937:         ))
1938:     }
1939: 
1940:     #[test]
1941:     fn test_pattern_matching() {
1942:         let backend = create_test_backend();
1943:         assert!(backend.matches_pattern("test-v1.0.0.zip", "test-*"));
1944:         assert!(!backend.matches_pattern("other-v1.0.0.zip", "test-*"));
1945:     }
1946: 
1947:     #[test]
1948:     fn test_version_prefix_functionality() {
1949:         let backend = create_test_backend();
1950:         let default_raw_opts = ToolVersionOptions::default();
1951:         let default_opts = backend.options(&default_raw_opts);
1952: 
1953:         // Test with no version prefix configured
1954:         assert_eq!(
1955:             backend.strip_version_prefix("v1.0.0", &default_opts),
1956:             "1.0.0"
1957:         );
1958:         assert_eq!(
1959:             backend.strip_version_prefix("1.0.0", &default_opts),
1960:             "1.0.0"
1961:         );
1962: 
1963:         // Test projectname@version format - only strips if prefix matches repo name
1964:         // Backend uses "github:test/repo" so repo short name is "repo", full name is "test/repo"
1965:         assert_eq!(
1966:             backend.strip_version_prefix("repo@0.15.0", &default_opts),
1967:             "0.15.0"
1968:         );
1969:         assert_eq!(
1970:             backend.strip_version_prefix("repo@1.2.3", &default_opts),
1971:             "1.2.3"
1972:         );
1973:         // Also accepts full repo name as prefix
1974:         assert_eq!(
1975:             backend.strip_version_prefix("test/repo@2.0.0", &default_opts),
1976:             "2.0.0"
1977:         );
1978:         // Should NOT strip if prefix doesn't match repo name (prevents listing
1979:         // versions that can't be installed)
1980:         assert_eq!(
1981:             backend.strip_version_prefix("other_package@0.15.0", &default_opts),
1982:             "other_package@0.15.0"
1983:         );
1984:         // Should not match if part after @ doesn't start with a digit
1985:         assert_eq!(
1986:             backend.strip_version_prefix("repo@beta", &default_opts),
1987:             "repo@beta"
1988:         );
1989: 
1990:         // Test with custom version prefix
1991:         let mut opts = ToolVersionOptions::default();
1992:         opts.opts.insert(
1993:             "version_prefix".to_string(),
1994:             toml::Value::String("release-".to_string()),
1995:         );
1996:         let opts = backend.options(&opts);
1997: 
1998:         assert_eq!(
1999:             backend.strip_version_prefix("release-1.0.0", &opts),
2000:             "1.0.0"
2001:         );
2002:         assert_eq!(backend.strip_version_prefix("1.0.0", &opts), "1.0.0");
2003:     }
2004: 
2005:     #[test]
2006:     fn test_find_asset_case_insensitive() {
2007:         let backend = create_test_backend();
2008: 
2009:         // Mock asset structs for testing
2010:         struct TestAsset {
2011:             name: String,
2012:         }
2013: 
2014:         let assets = vec![
2015:             TestAsset {
2016:                 name: "tool-1.0.0-linux-x86_64.tar.gz".to_string(),
2017:             },
2018:             TestAsset {
2019:                 name: "tool-1.0.0-Darwin-x86_64.tar.gz".to_string(),
2020:             },
2021:             TestAsset {
2022:                 name: "tool-1.0.0-Windows-x86_64.zip".to_string(),
2023:             },
2024:         ];
2025: 
2026:         // Test exact match (should find immediately)
2027:         let result =
2028:             backend.find_asset_case_insensitive(&assets, "tool-1.0.0-linux-x86_64.tar.gz", |a| {
2029:                 &a.name
2030:             });
2031:         assert!(result.is_some());
2032:         assert_eq!(result.unwrap().name, "tool-1.0.0-linux-x86_64.tar.gz");
2033: 
2034:         // Test case-insensitive match for Darwin (capital D)
2035:         let result = backend.find_asset_case_insensitive(
2036:             &assets,
2037:             "tool-1.0.0-darwin-x86_64.tar.gz", // lowercase 'd'
2038:             |a| &a.name,
2039:         );
2040:         assert!(result.is_some());
2041:         assert_eq!(result.unwrap().name, "tool-1.0.0-Darwin-x86_64.tar.gz");
2042: 
2043:         // Test case-insensitive match for Windows (capital W)
2044:         let result = backend.find_asset_case_insensitive(
2045:             &assets,
2046:             "tool-1.0.0-windows-x86_64.zip", // lowercase 'w'
2047:             |a| &a.name,
2048:         );
2049:         assert!(result.is_some());
2050:         assert_eq!(result.unwrap().name, "tool-1.0.0-Windows-x86_64.zip");
2051: 
2052:         // Test no match
2053:         let result =
2054:             backend.find_asset_case_insensitive(&assets, "nonexistent-asset.tar.gz", |a| &a.name);
2055:         assert!(result.is_none());
2056:     }

2081:     #[test]

1: //! Sole mise-internal bridge to the `mise-sigstore` crate.
2: //!
3: //! Every call mise makes into `mise_sigstore` goes through this module. Callers never
4: //! touch the underlying crate directly and never pass a GitHub token — the token is resolved
5: //! internally via [`crate::github::resolve_token_for_api_url`], which walks the full chain
6: //! (env vars → `credential_command` → `github_tokens.toml` → gh CLI → git credential fill).
7: //!
8: //! ## Why this exists
9: //!
10: //! Before this module, three sigstore call sites (`src/backend/aqua.rs`,
11: //! `src/plugins/core/python.rs`, `src/plugins/core/ruby.rs`) passed
12: //! `crate::env::GITHUB_TOKEN.as_deref()` — env vars only — while the github backend used the
13: //! full chain. That asymmetry left `mise lock` issuing unauthenticated attestation requests,
14: //! which hit GitHub's 60/hour IP rate limit after the second run.
15: //!
16: //! Concentrating the `mise_sigstore` surface here makes the asymmetry
17: //! structurally impossible: wrapper signatures omit the token argument, so callers cannot
18: //! re-introduce the bug without first editing this file.
19: //!
20: //! ## Default API URL
21: //!
22: //! Functions that accept `api_url: Option<&str>` fall back to [`crate::github::API_URL`]
23: //! (`"https://api.github.com"`) when `None` is passed. The default must be a full URL so
24: //! [`crate::github::resolve_token_for_api_url`] can parse the host correctly; a bare hostname
25: //! would be silently misrouted for GitHub Enterprise Server tenants.
26: 
27: use std::path::Path;
28: 
29: use mise_sigstore::sources::github::GitHubSource;
30: use mise_sigstore::{ArtifactRef, AttestationSource};
31: 
32: pub use mise_sigstore::AttestationError;
33: 
34: /// Result alias that matches `mise_sigstore`'s internal convention.
35: type AttestationResult<T> = std::result::Result<T, AttestationError>;
36: 
37: /// Resolve a GitHub token for an optional API base URL, defaulting to [`crate::github::API_URL`].
38: fn resolve_token_for_wrapper(api_url: Option<&str>) -> Option<String> {
39:     let url = api_url.unwrap_or(crate::github::API_URL);
40:     crate::github::resolve_token_for_api_url(url)
41: }
42: 
43: /// Verify a GitHub artifact attestation for a file on disk.
44: ///
45: /// Dispatches to [`mise_sigstore::verify_github_attestation_with_base_url`] when
46: /// `api_url` is `Some` (to support GitHub Enterprise) and to
47: /// [`mise_sigstore::verify_github_attestation`] otherwise.
48: pub async fn verify_attestation(
49:     artifact_path: &Path,
50:     owner: &str,
51:     repo: &str,
52:     expected_workflow: Option<&str>,
53:     api_url: Option<&str>,
54: ) -> AttestationResult<bool> {
55:     let token = resolve_token_for_wrapper(api_url);
56:     match api_url {
57:         Some(base_url) => {
58:             mise_sigstore::verify_github_attestation_with_base_url(
59:                 artifact_path,
60:                 owner,
61:                 repo,
62:                 token.as_deref(),
63:                 expected_workflow,
64:                 base_url,
65:             )
66:             .await
67:         }
68:         None => {
69:             mise_sigstore::verify_github_attestation(
70:                 artifact_path,
71:                 owner,
72:                 repo,
73:                 token.as_deref(),
74:                 expected_workflow,
75:             )
76:             .await
77:         }
78:     }
79: }
80: 
81: /// Reason the pre-download attestation probe could not complete.
82: ///
83: /// Preserved as two variants so callers can log distinct warnings for a misconfigured
84: /// endpoint (source creation) versus an API/network error (fetch). The original inline
85: /// pre-wrapper code at `src/backend/github.rs` emitted different messages for each; the
86: /// wrapper keeps that signal instead of flattening both into one error string.
87: #[derive(Debug)]
88: pub enum DetectError {
89:     /// `GitHubSource::with_base_url` rejected the (owner, repo, api_url) tuple — usually a
90:     /// malformed base URL.
91:     SourceCreation(AttestationError),
92:     /// The attestations endpoint returned an error (403 rate-limit, 5xx, network failure).
93:     Fetch(AttestationError),
94: }
95: 
96: impl std::fmt::Display for DetectError {
97:     fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
98:         match self {
99:             DetectError::SourceCreation(e) => write!(f, "{e}"),
100:             DetectError::Fetch(e) => write!(f, "{e}"),
101:         }
102:     }
103: }
104: 
105: impl std::error::Error for DetectError {
106:     fn source(&self) -> Option<&(dyn std::error::Error + 'static)> {
107:         match self {
108:             DetectError::SourceCreation(e) => Some(e),
109:             DetectError::Fetch(e) => Some(e),
110:         }
111:     }
112: }
113: 
114: /// Probe the GitHub attestation API for the given digest without downloading the artifact.
115: ///
116: /// Returns `Ok(true)` if any attestations exist for the digest. Used at lock time to decide
117: /// whether `ProvenanceType::GithubAttestations` should be recorded before committing to a
118: /// full download + verify.
119: pub async fn detect_attestations(
120:     owner: &str,
121:     repo: &str,
122:     api_url: &str,
123:     digest: &str,
124: ) -> Result<bool, DetectError> {
125:     let token = resolve_token_for_wrapper(Some(api_url));
126:     let source = GitHubSource::with_base_url(owner, repo, token.as_deref(), api_url)
127:         .map_err(DetectError::SourceCreation)?;
128:     let artifact_ref = ArtifactRef::from_digest(digest);
129:     let attestations = source
130:         .fetch_attestations(&artifact_ref)
131:         .await
132:         .map_err(DetectError::Fetch)?;
133:     Ok(!attestations.is_empty())
134: }
135: 
136: /// Verify SLSA provenance for an already-downloaded artifact. Passthrough — no token needed.
137: pub async fn verify_slsa_provenance(
138:     artifact_path: &Path,
139:     provenance_path: &Path,
140:     min_level: u8,
141: ) -> AttestationResult<bool> {
142:     mise_sigstore::verify_slsa_provenance(artifact_path, provenance_path, min_level).await
143: }
144: 
145: /// Verify a keyless Cosign signature or bundle. Passthrough — no token needed.
146: pub async fn verify_cosign_signature(
147:     artifact_path: &Path,
148:     sig_or_bundle_path: &Path,
149: ) -> AttestationResult<bool> {
150:     mise_sigstore::verify_cosign_signature(artifact_path, sig_or_bundle_path).await
151: }
152: 
153: /// Verify a Cosign signature against a public key. Passthrough — no token needed.
154: pub async fn verify_cosign_signature_with_key(
155:     artifact_path: &Path,
156:     sig_or_bundle_path: &Path,
157:     public_key_path: &Path,
158: ) -> AttestationResult<bool> {
159:     mise_sigstore::verify_cosign_signature_with_key(
160:         artifact_path,
161:         sig_or_bundle_path,
162:         public_key_path,
163:     )
164:     .await
165: }
166: 
167: #[cfg(test)]
168: mod tests {
169:     use super::*;
170:     use crate::env as mise_env;
171: 
172:     const TOKEN_ENV_VARS: &[&str] = &[
173:         "MISE_GITHUB_TOKEN",
174:         "GITHUB_API_TOKEN",
175:         "GITHUB_TOKEN",
176:         "MISE_GITHUB_ENTERPRISE_TOKEN",
177:     ];
178: 
179:     /// RAII guard: snapshots the tracked token env vars on construction, clears them for the
180:     /// test body, and restores the original values on drop — including when a test panics.
181:     struct TokenEnvGuard {
182:         saved: Vec<(&'static str, Option<String>)>,
183:     }
184: 
185:     impl TokenEnvGuard {
186:         fn new() -> Self {
187:             let saved: Vec<_> = TOKEN_ENV_VARS
188:                 .iter()
189:                 .map(|name| (*name, std::env::var(name).ok()))
190:                 .collect();
191:             for name in TOKEN_ENV_VARS {
192:                 mise_env::remove_var(name);
193:             }
194:             Self { saved }
195:         }
196:     }
197: 
198:     impl Drop for TokenEnvGuard {
199:         fn drop(&mut self) {
200:             for (name, value) in std::mem::take(&mut self.saved) {
201:                 match value {
202:                     Some(v) => mise_env::set_var(name, v),
203:                     None => mise_env::remove_var(name),
204:                 }
205:             }
206:         }
207:     }
208: 
209:     #[test]
210:     fn test_resolve_token_wrapper_uses_env_var_with_default_url() {
211:         let _lock = crate::github::TEST_ENV_LOCK.lock().unwrap();

215:         let resolved = resolve_token_for_wrapper(None);

225:         let _lock = crate::github::TEST_ENV_LOCK.lock().unwrap();

228: 
229:         let resolved = resolve_token_for_wrapper(Some(crate::github::API_URL));
230:         assert_eq!(
231:             resolved.as_deref(),
232:             Some("ghp_explicit_api"),
233:             "explicit api.github.com URL should resolve identically to the default"
234:         );
235:     }
236:

239:         let _lock = crate::github::TEST_ENV_LOCK.lock().unwrap();

For archives where individual files inside archive are attested, we need to extract archive and verify each file inside? Or maybe just verify that the inner binary (the file being installed) has attestation. We need check sigstore verify_attestation can accept a file path; it likely verifies a blob against GitHub attestation API based on digest of blob. For archive-level attestations, the release attestation is for the archive blob. For file-inside-archive attestations, the attestation is for each extracted file. Since the archive must be extracted before verification, we need perhaps extract to a temporary dir first, then verify each extracted file against GitHub attestation API, or perhaps identify the intended binary file(s) and verify them. But the current flow: download archive, verify checksum, then verify attestations/SLSA, then install_artifact extracts archive into install path. The request: "Some GitHub releases attest each file inside an archive rather than the archive blob itself. Make mise accept those safely." So we need to support after downloading archive, if archive-level attestation not found, try extracting to a temporary dir and verifying each file inside archive. If all files verified, then accept; if some fail, fail closed. Could then move extracted files to install path.

But we need preserve archive-level verification. The fallback should fail closed. The phrase "safely" and "fail closed" means we cannot accept unverified files. If the fallback can't verify inner files (e.g., no attestation for them, or mismatch), we must reject.

We need to think about scope. The user asks to implement this. We need ask consequential trust-boundary questions. But what? Possibly ask whether to accept partial archive internal attestation when only some inner files are attested (e.g., only binary, not metadata). The user says "accept those safely" and "fail closed". We should ask to define the policy: verify every file in the archive, or just the executable files? Or skip non-executable files? Also ask about signature: attestation API returns digest of each file? For GitHub artifact attestations, the attestation is for a subject digest. The file inside archive is an entry. For the attestation API, we need digest of the file to query. The verifier (mise-sigstore) likely uses file path and computes digest. It can verify any file. So we can extract the archive and verify each entry. If the archive contains many files, including text, metadata, and all are attested? In some releases (like cosign releases?), each binary and checksum file is attested individually? The issue may be about releases where the archive (e.g., tar.gz) contains files each with .sigstore.json attestation assets. But GitHub attestation API is not per-file sigstore.json? Wait, GitHub artifact attestations are stored via API and bound to repository. The release may attach .sigstore.json files for each file inside archive. The `verify_attestation` in mise-sigstore likely expects either the artifact file to have a sidecar .sigstore.json or uses GitHub API? Let's understand: `mise_sigstore::verify_github_attestation(artifact_path, owner, repo, token, expected_workflow)` likely queries GitHub attestation API for the artifact's digest. The GitHub attestation API returns attestations for the subject digest. So if the release attested each file inside the archive (subject digest per file), the archive blob digest won't match; but individual files digest will. To verify, we need to extract the archive and compute digest of each file, then verify with API. But we can call verify_attestation on each extracted file.

However, there are also sigstore bundles as assets (.sigstore.json) that can verify any file. The current `verify_attestation` may not use sidecar files; it uses GitHub API. Need inspect mise-sigstore crate? Not in source. Could find docs/cargo. Let's see Cargo.toml dependencies.

17: #[derive(Debug, Error)]
18: pub enum AttestationError {

55: pub type Result<T> = std::result::Result<T, AttestationError>;
56: 
57: #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
58: pub struct ArtifactRef {
59:     digest: String,
60: }
61: 
62: impl ArtifactRef {
63:     pub fn from_digest(digest: &str) -> Self {
64:         if digest.contains(':') {
65:             Self {
66:                 digest: digest.to_string(),
67:             }
68:         } else {
69:             Self {
70:                 digest: format!("sha256:{digest}"),
71:             }
72:         }
73:     }
74: }
75: 
76: #[async_trait]
77: pub trait AttestationSource {
78:     async fn fetch_attestations(&self, artifact: &ArtifactRef) -> Result<Vec<Attestation>>;
79: }
80:

83:

86:     }
87: }
88: 
89: #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
90: pub struct GitHubSource {
91:     client: AttestationClient,
92:     owner: String,
93:     repo: String,
94: }
95: 
96: impl GitHubSource {
97:     pub fn new(owner: &str, repo: &str, token: Option<&str>) -> Result<Self> {
98:         let mut builder = AttestationClient::builder();
99:         if let Some(token) = token {
100:             builder = builder.github_token(token);
101:         }
102:         Ok(Self {
103:             client: builder.build()?,
104:             owner: owner.to_string(),
105:             repo: repo.to_string(),
106:         })
107:     }
108: 
109:     pub fn with_base_url(
110:         owner: &str,
111:         repo: &str,
112:         token: Option<&str>,
113:         base_url: &str,
114:     ) -> Result<Self> {
115:         let mut builder = AttestationClient::builder().base_url(base_url);
116:         if let Some(token) = token {
117:             builder = builder.github_token(token);
118:         }
119:         Ok(Self {
120:             client: builder.build()?,
121:             owner: owner.to_string(),
122:             repo: repo.to_string(),
123:         })
124:     }
125: }
