127: #[async_trait]
128: impl AttestationSource for GitHubSource {
129:     async fn fetch_attestations(&self, artifact: &ArtifactRef) -> Result<Vec<Attestation>> {
130:         self.client
131:             .fetch_attestations(FetchParams {
132:                 owner: self.owner.clone(),
133:                 repo: Some(format!("{}/{}", self.owner, self.repo)),
134:                 digest: artifact.digest.clone(),
135:                 limit: 30,
136:                 predicate_type: None,
137:             })
138:             .await
139:     }
140: }
141: 
142: #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
143: pub struct AttestationClient {
144:     client: reqwest::Client,
145:     base_url: String,
146:     github_token: Option<String>,
147: }
148: 
149: #[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]
150: pub struct AttestationClientBuilder {
151:     base_url: Option<String>,
152:     github_token: Option<String>,
153: }
154: 
155: impl AttestationClientBuilder {
156:     pub fn base_url(mut self, url: &str) -> Self {
157:         self.base_url = Some(url.trim_end_matches('/').to_string());
158:         self
159:     }
160: 
161:     pub fn github_token(mut self, token: &str) -> Self {
162:         self.github_token = Some(token.to_string());
163:         self
164:     }
165: 
166:     pub fn build(self) -> Result<AttestationClient> {
167:         let mut headers = HeaderMap::new();
168:         headers.insert(USER_AGENT, HeaderValue::from_static(USER_AGENT_VALUE));
169:         let client = reqwest::Client::builder()
170:             .default_headers(headers)
171:             .build()?;
172: 
173:         Ok(AttestationClient {
174:             client,
175:             base_url: self.base_url.unwrap_or_else(|| GITHUB_API_URL.to_string()),
176:             github_token: self.github_token,
177:         })
178:     }
179: }
180: 
181: #[derive(Debug, Serialize)]
182: pub struct FetchParams {
183:     pub owner: String,
184:     pub repo: Option<String>,
185:     pub digest: String,
186:     pub limit: usize,
187:     pub predicate_type: Option<String>,
188: }
189: 
190: #[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]
191: struct AttestationsResponse {
192:     attestations: Vec<Attestation>,
193: }
194: 
195: #[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize)]
196: pub struct Attestation {
197:     bundle: Option<serde_json::Value>,
198:     bundle_url: Option<String>,
199: }
200: 
201: impl AttestationClient {
202:     pub fn builder() -> AttestationClientBuilder {
203:         AttestationClientBuilder::default()
204:     }
205: 
206:     fn github_headers(&self, url: &str) -> Result<HeaderMap> {
207:         let mut headers = HeaderMap::new();
208:         let base_with_slash = format!("{}/", self.base_url);
209:         if url == self.base_url || url.starts_with(&base_with_slash) {
210:             if let Some(token) = &self.github_token {
211:                 headers.insert(
212:                     AUTHORIZATION,
213:                     HeaderValue::from_str(&format!("Bearer {token}"))
214:                         .map_err(|e| AttestationError::Api(e.to_string()))?,
215:                 );
216:             }
217:             headers.insert(
218:                 "x-github-api-version",
219:                 HeaderValue::from_static("2022-11-28"),
220:             );
221:         }
222:         Ok(headers)
223:     }
224: 
225:     pub async fn fetch_attestations(&self, params: FetchParams) -> Result<Vec<Attestation>> {
226:         let url = if let Some(repo) = &params.repo {
227:             format!(
228:                 "{}/repos/{repo}/attestations/{}",
229:                 self.base_url, params.digest
230:             )
231:         } else {
232:             format!(
233:                 "{}/orgs/{}/attestations/{}",
234:                 self.base_url, params.owner, params.digest
235:             )
236:         };
237: 
238:         let mut query_params = vec![("per_page", params.limit.to_string())];
239:         if let Some(predicate_type) = &params.predicate_type {
240:             query_params.push(("predicate_type", predicate_type.clone()));
241:         }
242:         let url = reqwest::Url::parse_with_params(&url, query_params)
243:             .map_err(|e| AttestationError::Api(format!("Invalid GitHub attestations URL: {e}")))?;
244: 
245:         let response = self
246:             .client
247:             .get(url.clone())
248:             .headers(self.github_headers(url.as_str())?)
249:             .send()
250:             .await?;
251: 
252:         if response.status() == reqwest::StatusCode::NOT_FOUND {
253:             return Ok(vec![]);
254:         }
255:         if !response.status().is_success() {
256:             let status = response.status();
257:             let body = response
258:                 .text()
259:                 .await
260:                 .unwrap_or_else(|_| "Unknown error".to_string());
261:             return Err(AttestationError::Api(format!(
262:                 "GitHub API returned {status}: {body}"
263:             )));
264:         }
265: 
266:         let response: AttestationsResponse = response.json().await?;
267:         let mut attestations = Vec::new();
268:         for attestation in response.attestations {
269:             if attestation.bundle.is_some() {
270:                 attestations.push(attestation);
271:             } else if let Some(bundle_url) = &attestation.bundle_url {
272:                 let bundle = self.fetch_bundle_url(bundle_url).await?;
273:                 attestations.push(Attestation {
274:                     bundle: Some(bundle),
275:                     bundle_url: Some(bundle_url.clone()),
276:                 });
277:             }
278:         }
279:         Ok(attestations)
280:     }
281: 
282:     async fn fetch_bundle_url(&self, bundle_url: &str) -> Result<serde_json::Value> {
283:         let response = self
284:             .client
285:             .get(bundle_url)
286:             .headers(self.github_headers(bundle_url)?)
287:             .send()
288:             .await?;
289:         if !response.status().is_success() {
290:             return Err(AttestationError::Api(format!(
291:                 "bundle URL returned {}",
292:                 response.status()
293:             )));
294:         }
295:         if is_snappy_content_type(&response) {
296:             let bytes = response.bytes().await?;
297:             let decompressed = snap::raw::Decoder::new()
298:                 .decompress_vec(&bytes)
299:                 .map_err(|e| AttestationError::Api(format!("Snappy decompression failed: {e}")))?;
300:             serde_json::from_slice(&decompressed).map_err(AttestationError::Json)
301:         } else {
302:             response.json().await.map_err(AttestationError::Http)
303:         }
304:     }
305: }
306: 
307: pub async fn verify_github_attestation(
308:     artifact_path: &Path,
309:     owner: &str,
310:     repo: &str,
311:     token: Option<&str>,
312:     signer_workflow: Option<&str>,
313: ) -> Result<bool> {
314:     verify_github_attestation_inner(artifact_path, owner, repo, token, signer_workflow, None).await
315: }
316: 
317: pub async fn verify_github_attestation_with_base_url(
318:     artifact_path: &Path,
319:     owner: &str,
320:     repo: &str,
321:     token: Option<&str>,
322:     signer_workflow: Option<&str>,
323:     base_url: &str,
324: ) -> Result<bool> {
325:     verify_github_attestation_inner(
326:         artifact_path,
327:         owner,
328:         repo,
329:         token,
330:         signer_workflow,
331:         Some(base_url),
332:     )
333:     .await
334: }
335: 
336: async fn verify_github_attestation_inner(
337:     artifact_path: &Path,
338:     owner: &str,
339:     repo: &str,
340:     token: Option<&str>,
341:     signer_workflow: Option<&str>,
342:     base_url: Option<&str>,
343: ) -> Result<bool> {
344:     let mut builder = AttestationClient::builder();
345:     if let Some(token) = token {
346:         builder = builder.github_token(token);
347:     }
348:     if let Some(base_url) = base_url {
349:         builder = builder.base_url(base_url);
350:     }
351:     let client = builder.build()?;
352:     let digest = calculate_file_digest_async(artifact_path).await?;
353:     let attestations = client
354:         .fetch_attestations(FetchParams {
355:             owner: owner.to_string(),
356:             repo: Some(format!("{owner}/{repo}")),
357:             digest: format!("sha256:{digest}"),
358:             limit: 30,
359:             predicate_type: None,
360:         })
361:         .await?;
362: 
363:     if attestations.is_empty() {
364:         return Err(AttestationError::NoAttestations);
365:     }
366: 
367:     let artifact = tokio::fs::read(artifact_path).await?;
368:     let mut trust_roots = TrustRoots::default();
369:     verify_attestation_bundles(&attestations, &artifact, signer_workflow, &mut trust_roots).await
370: }
371: 
372: pub async fn verify_cosign_signature(
373:     artifact_path: &Path,
374:     sig_or_bundle_path: &Path,
375: ) -> Result<bool> {
376:     let content = tokio::fs::read_to_string(sig_or_bundle_path).await?;
377:     let artifact = tokio::fs::read(artifact_path).await?;
378:     let mut trust_roots = TrustRoots::default();
379:     if let Ok(bundle) = Bundle::from_json(&content) {
380:         let trusted_root = trust_roots.for_bundle(&bundle).await?;
381:         verify_bundle(&artifact, &bundle, None, trusted_root)?;
382:         return Ok(true);
383:     }
384:     // Legacy cosign v1 bundle (`{base64Signature, cert, rekorBundle}`).
385:     // sigstore-verify only consumes the modern bundle shape, so we verify
386:     // these manually: chain-validate the embedded cert against Sigstore
387:     // Fulcio, then ECDSA-verify the signature over the artifact bytes.
388:     let trusted_root = trust_roots.sigstore_root().await?;
389:     verify_legacy_cosign_bundle(&artifact, &content, trusted_root)?;
390:     Ok(true)
391: }
392: 
393: pub async fn verify_cosign_signature_with_key(
394:     artifact_path: &Path,
395:     sig_or_bundle_path: &Path,
396:     public_key_path: &Path,
397: ) -> Result<bool> {
398:     let key_pem = tokio::fs::read_to_string(public_key_path).await?;
399:     let public_key = DerPublicKey::from_pem(&key_pem)?;
400: 
401:     // Read the file once, propagating real I/O errors. Only a JSON-parse
402:     // failure means "this isn't a sigstore bundle, treat it as a raw `.sig`."
403:     let raw_bytes = tokio::fs::read(sig_or_bundle_path).await?;
404:     let bundle = std::str::from_utf8(&raw_bytes)
405:         .ok()
406:         .and_then(|content| Bundle::from_json(content).ok());
407:     if let Some(bundle) = bundle {
408:         // Bundle path: needs the trust root for tlog (Rekor) verification.
409:         let trusted_root = production_trusted_root().await?;
410:         let artifact = tokio::fs::read(artifact_path).await?;
411:         let result = sigstore_verify::verify_with_key(
412:             artifact.as_slice(),
413:             &bundle,
414:             &public_key,
415:             &trusted_root,
416:         )?;
417:         if !result.success {
418:             return Err(AttestationError::Verification(
419:                 "sigstore verification returned false".to_string(),
420:             ));
421:         }
422:         return Ok(true);
423:     }
424: 
425:     // Raw `.sig` path: only needs the local public key — no network access.
426:     let artifact = tokio::fs::read(artifact_path).await?;
427:     let signature = decode_cosign_signature(&raw_bytes);
428:     verify_raw_signature(&artifact, &signature, &public_key)?;
429:     Ok(true)
430: }
431: 
432: pub async fn verify_slsa_provenance(
433:     artifact_path: &Path,
434:     provenance_path: &Path,
435:     min_level: u8,
436: ) -> Result<bool> {
437:     let artifact = tokio::fs::read(artifact_path).await?;
438:     let content = tokio::fs::read_to_string(provenance_path).await?;
439:     let mut errors = Vec::new();
440:     let mut trust_roots = TrustRoots::default();
441: 
442:     let mut candidates: Vec<&str> = content
443:         .lines()
444:         .map(str::trim)
445:         .filter(|line| !line.is_empty())
446:         .collect();
447:     let trimmed = content.trim();
448:     if !trimmed.is_empty() && !candidates.contains(&trimmed) {
449:         candidates.push(trimmed);
450:     }
451: 
452:     for candidate in candidates {
453:         // Bundle::from_json failure falls through to the DSSE envelope path.
454:         if let Ok(bundle) = Bundle::from_json(candidate) {
455:             let result = match trust_roots.for_bundle(&bundle).await {
456:                 Ok(root) => verify_bundle(&artifact, &bundle, None, root)
457:                     .and_then(|_| verify_bundle_slsa(&bundle, &artifact, min_level)),
458:                 Err(e) => Err(e),
459:             };
460:             match result {
461:                 Ok(()) => return Ok(true),
462:                 Err(e) => errors.push(e),
463:             }
464:             continue;
465:         }
466:         // slsa-github-generator and goreleaser write the provenance as a raw
467:         // DSSE envelope (`*.intoto.jsonl`) rather than a sigstore bundle —
468:         // there is no `verificationMaterial`, so `Bundle::from_json` rejects
469:         // it. Match the in-toto payload manually and check artifact digest +
470:         // SLSA predicate without going through sigstore-verify. Use the public
471:         // Sigstore trust root since slsa-github-generator certs are issued by
472:         // Sigstore Fulcio.
473:         let result = match trust_roots.sigstore_root().await {
474:             Ok(root) => verify_intoto_envelope(candidate, &artifact, min_level, root),
475:             Err(e) => Err(e),
476:         };
477:         match result {
478:             Ok(()) => return Ok(true),
479:             Err(e) => errors.push(e),
480:         }
481:     }
482: 
483:     collapse_slsa_errors(errors, || {
484:         "File does not contain valid attestations or SLSA provenance".to_string()
485:     })
486: }
487: 
488: fn verify_intoto_envelope(
489:     line: &str,
490:     artifact: &[u8],
491:     min_level: u8,
492:     trusted_root: &TrustedRoot,
493: ) -> Result<()> {
494:     let envelope: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(line).map_err(|e| {
495:         AttestationError::UnsupportedFormat(format!("not a JSON DSSE envelope: {e}"))
496:     })?;
497:     let payload_type = envelope
498:         .get("payloadType")
499:         .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
500:         .unwrap_or_default();
501:     if payload_type != "application/vnd.in-toto+json" {
502:         return Err(AttestationError::UnsupportedFormat(format!(
503:             "unsupported DSSE payloadType: {payload_type}"
504:         )));
505:     }
506:     let payload_b64 = envelope
507:         .get("payload")
508:         .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
509:         .ok_or_else(|| {
510:             AttestationError::UnsupportedFormat("DSSE envelope missing payload".to_string())
511:         })?;
512:     let payload = base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD
513:         .decode(payload_b64.as_bytes())
514:         .map_err(|e| AttestationError::Verification(format!("invalid base64 payload: {e}")))?;
515: 
516:     // DSSE signature verification. The envelope's signatures sign the
517:     // Pre-Authentication Encoding of the payload, not the payload itself.
518:     // Without this check, anyone able to substitute the provenance file could
519:     // forge a passing attestation just by including the artifact's digest in
520:     // the in-toto subject list.
521:     //
522:     // Each signature embeds the Sigstore Fulcio leaf cert that signed it
523:     // (slsa-github-generator format). We chain-validate that cert against the
524:     // public Sigstore trust root, then verify the signature against the PAE
525:     // using the cert's public key. A self-signed forged cert would be
526:     // rejected at the chain step. Bundles in the modern sigstore format
527:     // (which carry tlog/TSA) take the strict `verify_bundle` path above.
528:     let signatures = envelope
529:         .get("signatures")
530:         .and_then(|v| v.as_array())
531:         .ok_or_else(|| {
532:             AttestationError::Verification("DSSE envelope missing signatures".to_string())
533:         })?;
534:     if signatures.is_empty() {
535:         return Err(AttestationError::Verification(
536:             "DSSE envelope has no signatures".to_string(),
537:         ));
538:     }
539:     let pae = sigstore_verify::types::pae(payload_type, &payload);
540:     let mut sig_errors = Vec::new();
541:     let mut verified = false;
542:     for sig in signatures {
543:         match verify_dsse_signature(sig, &pae, trusted_root) {
544:             Ok(()) => {
545:                 verified = true;
546:                 break;
547:             }
548:             Err(e) => sig_errors.push(e.to_string()),
549:         }
550:     }
551:     if !verified {
552:         return Err(AttestationError::Verification(format!(
553:             "no valid DSSE signature: {}",
554:             join_error_strings(sig_errors, || "no signatures could be verified".to_string())
555:         )));
556:     }
557: 
558:     verify_intoto_payload(&payload, artifact, min_level)
559: }
560: 
561: /// Verify a legacy cosign v1 keyless bundle (`{base64Signature, cert, rekorBundle}`).
562: ///
563: /// Cosign 2.x and earlier `cosign sign-blob --bundle` writes this format. The
564: /// modern sigstore Bundle (with `verificationMaterial`/`messageSignature`)
565: /// replaces it, but tools like goreleaser still produce v1 bundles in their
566: /// release artifacts. Verification mirrors what we do for raw DSSE envelopes:
567: /// decode the embedded Fulcio cert (PEM in `cert`), chain-validate it against
568: /// the public Sigstore trust root, then ECDSA-verify `base64Signature` over
569: /// the raw artifact bytes with the cert's public key.
570: ///
571: /// The Rekor `SignedEntryTimestamp` and the artifact hash recorded in the
572: /// rekord entry aren't independently re-checked here — re-verifying them
573: /// would require a Rekor public key lookup and adds little: the cert+sig
574: /// step already cryptographically binds the signer to the artifact bytes,
575: /// which is what every downstream consumer cares about.
576: fn verify_legacy_cosign_bundle(
577:     artifact: &[u8],
578:     bundle_json: &str,
579:     trusted_root: &TrustedRoot,
580: ) -> Result<()> {
581:     let value: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(bundle_json).map_err(|e| {
582:         AttestationError::UnsupportedFormat(format!("not a sigstore or cosign bundle: {e}"))
583:     })?;
584:     let cert_b64 = value.get("cert").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).ok_or_else(|| {
585:         AttestationError::UnsupportedFormat("legacy cosign bundle missing cert".to_string())
586:     })?;
587:     let sig_b64 = value
588:         .get("base64Signature")
589:         .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
590:         .ok_or_else(|| {
591:             AttestationError::UnsupportedFormat(
592:                 "legacy cosign bundle missing base64Signature".to_string(),
593:             )
594:         })?;
595: 
596:     let cert_pem_bytes = base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD
597:         .decode(cert_b64.as_bytes())
598:         .map_err(|e| {
599:             AttestationError::Verification(format!("invalid base64 cert in legacy bundle: {e}"))
600:         })?;
601:     let cert_pem = std::str::from_utf8(&cert_pem_bytes).map_err(|e| {
602:         AttestationError::Verification(format!("legacy cosign cert is not UTF-8 PEM: {e}"))
603:     })?;
604:     let cert = DerCertificate::from_pem(cert_pem)?;
605:     verify_cert_chain(cert.as_bytes(), trusted_root)?;
606: 
607:     let sig_bytes = base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD
608:         .decode(sig_b64.as_bytes())
609:         .map_err(|e| AttestationError::Verification(format!("invalid base64 signature: {e}")))?;
610:     let spki_der = extract_spki_der(cert.as_bytes())?;
611:     let public_key = DerPublicKey::new(spki_der);
612:     verify_raw_signature(artifact, &sig_bytes, &public_key)
613: }
614: 
615: fn verify_dsse_signature(
616:     sig: &serde_json::Value,
617:     pae: &[u8],
618:     trusted_root: &TrustedRoot,
619: ) -> Result<()> {
620:     let cert_pem = sig.get("cert").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).ok_or_else(|| {
621:         AttestationError::Verification("DSSE signature missing cert field".to_string())
622:     })?;
623:     let sig_b64 = sig.get("sig").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).ok_or_else(|| {
624:         AttestationError::Verification("DSSE signature missing sig field".to_string())
625:     })?;
626:     let sig_bytes = base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD
627:         .decode(sig_b64.as_bytes())
628:         .map_err(|e| AttestationError::Verification(format!("invalid base64 signature: {e}")))?;
629:     let cert = DerCertificate::from_pem(cert_pem)?;
630:     // Chain-validate the embedded cert before trusting its public key.
631:     verify_cert_chain(cert.as_bytes(), trusted_root)?;
632:     let spki_der = extract_spki_der(cert.as_bytes())?;
633:     let public_key = DerPublicKey::new(spki_der);
634:     verify_raw_signature(pae, &sig_bytes, &public_key)
635: }
636: 
637: fn verify_intoto_payload(payload: &[u8], artifact: &[u8], min_level: u8) -> Result<()> {
638:     let statement: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_slice(payload).map_err(|e| {
639:         AttestationError::Verification(format!("Failed to parse SLSA payload: {e}"))
640:     })?;
641:     let predicate_type = statement
642:         .get("predicateType")
643:         .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
644:         .unwrap_or_default();
645:     if !predicate_type.starts_with("https://slsa.dev/provenance/") {
646:         return Err(AttestationError::UnsupportedFormat(format!(
647:             "Not an SLSA provenance predicate: {predicate_type}"
648:         )));
649:     }
650:     if min_level > 1 {
651:         return Err(AttestationError::Verification(format!(
652:             "SLSA level {min_level} verification is not supported by the native adapter"
653:         )));
654:     }
655:     let artifact_digest = hex::encode(Sha256::digest(artifact));
656:     let subjects = statement
657:         .get("subject")
658:         .and_then(|v| v.as_array())
659:         .ok_or_else(|| {
660:             AttestationError::Verification("SLSA statement missing subject array".to_string())
661:         })?;
662:     let matches_subject = subjects.iter().any(|subject| {
663:         subject
664:             .get("digest")
665:             .and_then(|d| d.get("sha256"))
666:             .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
667:             .is_some_and(|sha| sha.eq_ignore_ascii_case(&artifact_digest))
668:     });
669:     if !matches_subject {
670:         return Err(AttestationError::Verification(format!(
671:             "artifact sha256 {artifact_digest} not found in SLSA statement subjects"
672:         )));
673:     }
674:     Ok(())
675: }
676: 
677: fn collapse_slsa_errors(
678:     errors: Vec<AttestationError>,
679:     default: impl FnOnce() -> String,
680: ) -> Result<bool> {
681:     let unsupported_format = errors
682:         .iter()
683:         .all(|error| matches!(error, AttestationError::UnsupportedFormat(_)));
684:     let message = join_error_strings(
685:         errors.into_iter().map(|error| error.to_string()).collect(),
686:         default,
687:     );
688:     Err(if unsupported_format {
689:         AttestationError::UnsupportedFormat(message)
690:     } else {
691:         AttestationError::Verification(message)
692:     })
693: }
694: 
695: fn join_error_strings(errors: Vec<String>, default: impl FnOnce() -> String) -> String {
696:     let mut errors = errors
697:         .into_iter()
698:         .filter(|error| !error.trim().is_empty())
699:         .collect::<Vec<_>>();
700:     errors.dedup();
701:     if errors.is_empty() {
702:         default()
703:     } else {
704:         errors.join("; ")
705:     }
706: }
707: 
708: async fn verify_attestation_bundles(
709:     attestations: &[Attestation],
710:     artifact: &[u8],
711:     signer_workflow: Option<&str>,
712:     trust_roots: &mut TrustRoots,
713: ) -> Result<bool> {
714:     let mut errors = Vec::new();
715:     for attestation in attestations {
716:         let Some(bundle_value) = &attestation.bundle else {
717:             continue;
718:         };
719:         let bundle = match serde_json::from_value::<Bundle>(bundle_value.clone()) {
720:             Ok(bundle) => bundle,
721:             Err(e) => {
722:                 errors.push(e.to_string());
723:                 continue;
724:             }
725:         };
726:         let trusted_root = match trust_roots.for_bundle(&bundle).await {
727:             Ok(root) => root,
728:             Err(e) => {
729:                 errors.push(e.to_string());
730:                 continue;
731:             }
732:         };
733:         match verify_bundle(artifact, &bundle, signer_workflow, trusted_root) {
734:             Ok(()) => return Ok(true),
735:             Err(e) => errors.push(e.to_string()),
736:         }
737:     }
738: 
739:     Err(AttestationError::Verification(join_error_strings(
740:         errors,
741:         || "No valid attestations found".to_string(),
742:     )))
743: }
744: 
745: fn is_snappy_content_type(response: &reqwest::Response) -> bool {
746:     response
747:         .headers()
748:         .get(reqwest::header::CONTENT_TYPE)
749:         .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())
750:         .and_then(|content_type| content_type.split(';').next())
751:         .is_some_and(|content_type| content_type.trim() == "application/x-snappy")
752: }
753: 
754: fn verify_bundle(
755:     artifact: &[u8],
756:     bundle: &Bundle,
757:     signer_workflow: Option<&str>,
758:     trusted_root: &TrustedRoot,
759: ) -> Result<()> {
760:     let mut policy = VerificationPolicy::default();
761:     // sigstore-verify's default policy *requires* an inclusion proof when
762:     // `verify_tlog` is on. GitHub artifact attestations and TSA-only bundles
763:     // never carry one, so we'd reject them outright. Skip tlog only when the
764:     // bundle has no inclusion proof — public-Sigstore cosign bundles, which do
765:     // ship a Rekor inclusion proof, still get full tlog verification (Rekor
766:     // checkpoint signature, SET, inclusion-proof Merkle path).
767:     if !bundle.has_inclusion_proof() {
768:         policy = policy.skip_tlog();
769:     }
770:     // GitHub-internal leaf certs don't carry an SCT extension (GitHub's CA
771:     // doesn't log to public CT). `skip_sct` keeps full certificate-chain
772:     // validation against the GitHub trust root's Fulcio certs but turns off
773:     // the SCT check, which is exactly what GitHub artifact attestations need.
774:     if is_github_internal_certificate(bundle) {
775:         policy = policy.skip_sct();
776:     }
777:     let result = sigstore_verify::verify(artifact, bundle, &policy, trusted_root)?;
778:     if !result.success {
779:         return Err(AttestationError::Verification(
780:             "sigstore verification returned false".to_string(),
781:         ));
782:     }
783: 
784:     verify_signer_workflow_identity(result.identity.as_deref(), signer_workflow)?;
785: 
786:     Ok(())
787: }
788: 
789: fn is_github_internal_certificate(bundle: &Bundle) -> bool {
790:     bundle
791:         .signing_certificate()
792:         .map(|cert| cert_issuer_organization(cert.as_bytes()).as_deref() == Some("GitHub, Inc."))
793:         .unwrap_or(false)
794: }
795: 
796: /// Verify that a leaf certificate chains to one of the trust root's CA certs.
797: ///
798: /// Used for raw DSSE envelopes (`*.intoto.jsonl` from slsa-github-generator),
799: /// which don't have the bundle structure sigstore-verify expects, so we can't
800: /// delegate to `sigstore_verify::verify`. GitHub-internal bundles go through
801: /// sigstore-verify directly with `skip_sct`.
802: ///
803: /// webpki performs the same chain-building, ECDSA/RSA signature checks, and
804: /// CODE_SIGNING EKU enforcement as sigstore-verify, just without the SCT step.
805: ///
806: /// Validation time is the leaf cert's `notAfter`. Fulcio leaves are
807: /// short-lived (~10 min) so by `now()` they're already expired and we have no
808: /// independently verified time source here. Using `notAfter` rather than
809: /// `notBefore` is the stricter choice: it catches any intermediate CA whose
810: /// own validity ends before the leaf's, which would otherwise slip through.
811: fn verify_cert_chain(leaf_der: &[u8], trusted_root: &TrustedRoot) -> Result<()> {
812:     use rustls_pki_types::{CertificateDer, UnixTime};
813:     use webpki::{ALL_VERIFICATION_ALGS, EndEntityCert, KeyUsage, anchor_from_trusted_cert};
814:     use x509_cert::Certificate;
815:     use x509_cert::der::Decode;
816: 
817:     let leaf = Certificate::from_der(leaf_der).map_err(|e| {
818:         AttestationError::Verification(format!("failed to parse leaf certificate: {e}"))
819:     })?;
820:     let not_after = leaf
821:         .tbs_certificate
822:         .validity
823:         .not_after
824:         .to_unix_duration()
825:         .as_secs();
826:     let validation_time = UnixTime::since_unix_epoch(std::time::Duration::from_secs(not_after));
827: 
828:     let all_certs = trusted_root.fulcio_certs().map_err(|e| {
829:         AttestationError::Verification(format!("failed to load CA certs from trust root: {e}"))
830:     })?;
831:     if all_certs.is_empty() {
832:         return Err(AttestationError::Verification(
833:             "trust root contains no CA certificates".to_string(),
834:         ));
835:     }
836:     // Use every CA cert in the trust root as both a trust anchor and as a
837:     // possible intermediate. `anchor_from_trusted_cert` accepts any parseable
838:     // cert (not just self-signed roots), and that's intentional: we trust the
839:     // whole CA bundle the trust root ships, so it's fine for chain validation
840:     // to terminate at an intermediate rather than walk all the way up to the
841:     // self-signed root. This matches what sigstore-verify does internally.
842:     // The chain itself is still cryptographically verified end-to-end.
843:     let trust_anchors: Vec<_> = all_certs
844:         .iter()
845:         .filter_map(|der| {
846:             anchor_from_trusted_cert(&CertificateDer::from(der.as_ref()))
847:                 .map(|a| a.to_owned())
848:                 .ok()
849:         })
850:         .collect();
851:     if trust_anchors.is_empty() {
852:         return Err(AttestationError::Verification(
853:             "trust root CA certs are unparseable".to_string(),
854:         ));
855:     }
856:     let intermediate_certs: Vec<CertificateDer<'static>> = all_certs
857:         .iter()
858:         .map(|der| CertificateDer::from(der.as_ref()).into_owned())
859:         .collect();
860: 
861:     let leaf_der_ref = CertificateDer::from(leaf_der);
862:     let leaf_cert = EndEntityCert::try_from(&leaf_der_ref).map_err(|e| {
863:         AttestationError::Verification(format!("failed to parse leaf for chain check: {e}"))
864:     })?;
865: 
866:     // 1.3.6.1.5.5.7.3.3 — id-kp-codeSigning, raw OID bytes (no DER tag/length).
867:     const ID_KP_CODE_SIGNING: &[u8] = &[0x2b, 0x06, 0x01, 0x05, 0x05, 0x07, 0x03, 0x03];
868: 
869:     leaf_cert
870:         .verify_for_usage(
871:             ALL_VERIFICATION_ALGS,
872:             &trust_anchors,
873:             &intermediate_certs,
874:             validation_time,
875:             KeyUsage::required(ID_KP_CODE_SIGNING),
876:             None,
877:             None,
878:         )
879:         .map_err(|e| {
880:             AttestationError::Verification(format!("certificate chain validation failed: {e}"))
881:         })?;
882:     Ok(())
883: }

885: /// Return the X.509 Issuer's `O` (organizationName) attribute, if present.
886: ///
887: /// Used to dispatch verification policy: certs issued by GitHub's internal
888: /// Fulcio (`O=GitHub, Inc.`) need a separate trust root and a relaxed policy.
889: /// Parses the cert with x509-cert rather than byte-searching the DER, so we
890: /// only match the actual issuer organization field — not arbitrary substrings
891: /// elsewhere in the certificate.
892: fn cert_issuer_organization(cert_der: &[u8]) -> Option<String> {
893:     use x509_cert::Certificate;
894:     use x509_cert::der::Decode;
895:     let cert = Certificate::from_der(cert_der).ok()?;
896:     for rdn in cert.tbs_certificate.issuer.0.iter() {
897:         for atv in rdn.0.iter() {
898:             // 2.5.4.10 = id-at-organizationName
899:             if atv.oid.to_string() == "2.5.4.10" {
900:                 if let Ok(s) = atv.value.decode_as::<String>() {
901:                     return Some(s);
902:                 }
903:                 if let Ok(s) = atv
904:                     .value
905:                     .decode_as::<x509_cert::der::asn1::PrintableStringRef>()
906:                 {
907:                     return Some(s.as_str().to_string());
908:                 }
909:                 if let Ok(s) = atv.value.decode_as::<x509_cert::der::asn1::Utf8StringRef>() {
910:                     return Some(s.as_str().to_string());
911:                 }
912:             }
913:         }
914:     }
915:     None
916: }

918: /// Extract the SubjectPublicKeyInfo bytes (DER) from an X.509 certificate.
919: fn extract_spki_der(cert_der: &[u8]) -> Result<Vec<u8>> {
920:     use x509_cert::Certificate;
921:     use x509_cert::der::{Decode, Encode};
922:     let cert = Certificate::from_der(cert_der)
923:         .map_err(|e| AttestationError::Verification(format!("failed to parse certificate: {e}")))?;
924:     cert.tbs_certificate
925:         .subject_public_key_info
926:         .to_der()
927:         .map_err(|e| {
928:             AttestationError::Verification(format!("failed to encode SubjectPublicKeyInfo: {e}"))
929:         })
930: }

940: /// Per-process cache so we only fetch the Sigstore TUF root or parse the
941: /// embedded GitHub trusted root once per `verify_*` invocation. Each is
942: /// loaded lazily — a verification flow that only ever sees GitHub bundles
943: /// never triggers a network call to the Sigstore TUF CDN, and vice versa.
944: #[derive(Default)]
945: struct TrustRoots {
946:     sigstore: Option<TrustedRoot>,
947:     github: Option<TrustedRoot>,

950: impl TrustRoots {

966:     fn github_root(&mut self) -> Result<&TrustedRoot> {

969:         }

982:         return Err(AttestationError::Verification(format!(

984:         )));

988:             "Workflow verification failed: expected '{expected}', found certificate identity: {identity:?}"
989:         )));

994: /// SLSA-specific checks once `verify_bundle` has cryptographically verified
995: /// the bundle: the DSSE payload is an SLSA provenance statement, the policy
996: /// level is supported, and the artifact's SHA-256 appears in the statement's
997: /// `subject` array. The subject check is the load-bearing part — without it,
998: /// a valid SLSA bundle signed for *some* artifact would accept *any* artifact.
999: fn verify_bundle_slsa(bundle: &Bundle, artifact: &[u8], min_level: u8) -> Result<()> {
1000:     let payload = match &bundle.content {
1001:         sigstore_verify::types::SignatureContent::DsseEnvelope(envelope) => {
1002:             envelope.decode_payload()
1003:         }
1004:         _ => {
1005:             return Err(AttestationError::UnsupportedFormat(
1006:                 "SLSA provenance must be a DSSE envelope".to_string(),
1007:             ));
1008:         }
1009:     };

1016:         .decode(trimmed.as_bytes())
1017:         .ok()

1020:         return decoded;

1054:         }
1055:         hasher.update(&buffer[..read]);
1056:     }

state.title: crates/mise-sigstore/src/lib.rs

part.text:
The mise-sigstore verify_github_attestation uses file digest via GitHub API. So we can call it for each extracted file. For NoAttestations it returns error. Need determine if "each file inside an archive" means all entries. We need implement fallback in github backend's attestation verification: if archive-level verification returns NoAttestations (not failure) and the file is an archive, extract it to a temp directory, then try to verify each entry. If all entries have attestations and verified, accept. If any entry fails verification, hard error. If no archive or some entries no attestations, return NoAttestations to fall back to SLSA (or fail closed if lockfile expects attestations). But the fallback must fail closed: if lockfile expects github-attestations and archive-level NoAttestations but inner files exist and some unverified -> fail, not silently accept. However if lockfile expects nothing, and archive-level no attestation, but inner files can be verified, record provenance. That is new.

Need inspect install_artifact (static_helpers) to understand archive extraction. Also maybe create helper to extract to temp. There may already be extract_archive etc. Read static_helpers around install_artifact and extract.

113: where

121: /// Helper to try various tag formats for a resolver function
122: /// Tries version_prefix (if set), v prefix, and optionally repo@version formats
123: pub async fn try_with_v_prefix_and_repo<F, Fut, T, E>(
124:     version: &str,
125:     version_prefix: Option<&str>,
126:     repo: Option<&str>,
127:     resolver: F,
128: ) -> Result<T>
129: where
130:     F: Fn(String) -> Fut,

139:         if version.starts_with(prefix) {
140:             vec![
141:                 version.to_string(),
142:                 version.trim_start_matches(prefix).to_string(),
143:             ]
144:         } else {
145:             vec![format!("{}{}", prefix, version), version.to_string()]
146:         }
147:     } else if version == "latest" {
148:         vec![version.to_string()]
149:     } else if version.starts_with('v') {
150:         vec![
151:             version.to_string(),
152:             version.trim_start_matches('v').to_string(),
153:         ]
154:     } else {
155:         vec![format!("v{version}"), version.to_string()]
156:     };

183:         }

220: /// Looks up a value in ToolVersionOptions using nested platform key format.
221: /// Supports nested format (platforms.macos-x64.url) with os-arch dash notation.
222: /// Also supports both "platforms" and "platform" prefixes.
223: pub fn lookup_platform_key(opts: &ToolVersionOptions, key_type: &str) -> Option<String> {
224:     // Try nested platform structure with os-arch format
225:     for (os, arch) in platform_aliases() {
226:         for prefix in ["platforms", "platform"] {
227:             // Try nested format: platforms.macos-x64.url
228:             let nested_key = format!("{prefix}.{os}-{arch}.{key_type}");
229:             if let Some(val) = opts.get_nested_string(&nested_key) {
230:                 return Some(val);

232:             // Try flat format: platforms_macos_arm64_url
233:             let flat_key = format!("{prefix}_{os}_{arch}_{key_type}");
234:             if let Some(val) = opts.get_string(&flat_key) {
235:                 return Some(val);

238:     }

310: /// Lists platform keys (e.g. "macos-x64") for which a given key_type exists (e.g. "url").
311: pub fn list_available_platforms_with_key(opts: &ToolVersionOptions, key_type: &str) -> Vec<String> {
312:     let mut set = IndexSet::new();
313: 
314:     // Gather from flat keys
315:     for (k, _) in opts.iter() {
316:         if let Some(rest) = k
317:             .strip_prefix("platforms_")
318:             .or_else(|| k.strip_prefix("platform_"))
319:             && let Some(platform_part) = rest.strip_suffix(&format!("_{}", key_type))
320:         {
321:             // Only convert the OS/arch separator underscore to a dash, preserving
322:             // underscores inside architecture names like x86_64
323:             let platform_key = if let Some((os_part, rest)) = platform_part.split_once('_') {
324:                 format!("{os_part}-{rest}")
325:             } else {
326:                 platform_part.to_string()
327:             };
328:             set.insert(platform_key);
329:         }
330:     }
331: 
332:     // Probe nested keys using shared patterns
333:     for os in OS_PATTERNS {
334:         for arch in ARCH_PATTERNS {
335:             for prefix in ["platforms", "platform"] {
336:                 let nested_key = format!("{prefix}.{os}-{arch}.{key_type}");
337:                 if opts.contains_key(&nested_key) {
338:                     set.insert(format!("{os}-{arch}"));
339:                 }
340:             }
341:         }
342:     }
343: 
344:     set.into_iter().collect()

347: pub fn template_string(template: &str, tv: &ToolVersion) -> String {
348:     // Check for legacy {version} syntax and emit deprecation warning
349:     if template.contains("{version}") && !template.contains("{{version}}") {
350:         deprecated_at!(
351:             "2026.3.0",
352:             "2027.3.0",
353:             "legacy-version-template",
354:             "Use {{{{ version }}}} instead of {{version}} in URL templates"
355:         );
356:         // Legacy support: replace {version} placeholder
357:         return template.replace("{version}", &tv.version);
358:     }
359: 
360:     if !crate::tera::contains_template_syntax(template) {
361:         return template.to_string();
362:     }
363: 
364:     // Use Tera rendering for templates
365:     // Supports {{ version }}, {{ os() }}, {{ arch() }}, etc.
366:     let mut ctx = crate::tera::BASE_CONTEXT.clone();
367:     ctx.insert("version", &tv.version);
368: 
369:     let mut tera = crate::tera::get_tera(None);
370:     match crate::tera::render_str(&mut tera, template, &ctx) {
371:         Ok(rendered) => rendered,
372:         Err(e) => {
373:             warn!("Failed to render template '{}': {}", template, e);
374:             template.to_string()
375:         }
376:     }
377: }

379: pub fn get_filename_from_url(url_str: &str) -> String {
380:     let filename = if let Ok(url) = url::Url::parse(url_str) {
381:         // Use proper URL parsing to get the path and extract filename
382:         url.path_segments()
383:             .and_then(|mut segments| segments.next_back())
384:             .map(|s| s.to_string())
385:             .unwrap_or_else(|| url_str.to_string())
386:     } else {

388:         url_str
389:             .split('/')
390:             .next_back()
391:             .unwrap_or(url_str)
392:             .to_string()
393:     };
394:     urlencoding::decode(&filename)
395:         .map(|s| s.to_string())
396:         .unwrap_or(filename)
397: }

449:         file::untar(

451:             &dest,
452:             &file::TarOptions {

454:                 ..file::TarOptions::new(format)

456:         )?;
457: 
458:         file::make_executable(&dest)?;
459:     } else if format == file::TarFormat::Raw {
460:         // Copy the file directly to the bin_path directory or install_path
461:         if let Some(bin_path_template) = lookup_with_fallback(opts, "bin_path") {
462:             let bin_path = template_string(&bin_path_template, tv);
463:             let bin_dir = install_path.join(&bin_path);

468:         } else if let Some(bin_name) = lookup_with_fallback(opts, "bin") {
469:             // If bin is specified, rename the file to this name

471:             file::copy(file_path, &dest)?;
472:             file::make_executable(&dest)?;
473:         } else {
474:             // Always auto-clean binary names by removing OS/arch suffixes
475:             let original_name = file_path.file_name().unwrap().to_string_lossy();
476:             let cleaned_name = clean_binary_name(&original_name, Some(&tv.ba().tool_name));
477:             let dest = install_path.join(cleaned_name);
478:             file::copy(file_path, &dest)?;
479:             file::make_executable(&dest)?;
480:         }
481:     } else {
482:         // Handle archive formats
483:         // Auto-detect if we need strip_components=1 before extracting
484:         // Only do this if strip_components was not explicitly set by the user AND bin_path is not configured
485:         if strip_components.is_none()
486:             && lookup_with_fallback(opts, "bin_path").is_none()
487:             && let Ok(should_strip) = file::should_strip_components(file_path, format)
488:             && should_strip
489:         {
490:             debug!("Auto-detected single directory archive, extracting with strip_components=1");
491:             strip_components = Some(1);
492:         }
493:         let tar_opts = file::TarOptions {
494:             strip_components: strip_components.unwrap_or(0),
495:             pr,
496:             ..file::TarOptions::new(format)
497:         };
498: 
499:         // Extract with determined strip_components
500:         file::untar(file_path, &install_path, &tar_opts)?;
501: 
502:         // Extract just the repo name from tool_name (e.g., "opsgenie/opsgenie-lamp" -> "opsgenie-lamp")
503:         let full_tool_name = tv.ba().tool_name.as_str();
504:         let tool_name = full_tool_name.rsplit('/').next().unwrap_or(full_tool_name);
505: 
506:         // Determine search directory based on bin_path option
507:         let explicit_bin_path = lookup_with_fallback(opts, "bin_path")
508:             .map(|t| install_path.join(template_string(&t, tv)));
509: 
510:         // Handle bin= option for archives (renames executable to specified name)
511:         // bin= values are relative to install_path, so always use install_path or explicit bin_path
512:         if let Some(bin_name) = lookup_with_fallback(opts, "bin") {
513:             let search_dir = explicit_bin_path.as_deref().unwrap_or(&install_path);
514:             make_configured_bin_executable(search_dir, &bin_name)?;

518:         // Handle rename_exe option for archives
519:         // When bin_path is not explicitly set, auto-detect bin/ subdirectory to match
520:         // the same logic used by discover_bin_paths() for PATH construction
521:         if let Some(rename_to) = lookup_with_fallback(opts, "rename_exe") {

524:             } else {

526:                 if bin_dir.is_dir() {
527:                     bin_dir
528:                 } else {
529:                     install_path.clone()
530:                 }

532:             rename_executable_in_dir(&search_dir, &rename_to, Some(tool_name))?;
533:         }
534:     }
535:     Ok(())
536: }

547:     _tv: &crate::toolset::ToolVersion,

596: /// Checks if a file should be skipped when searching for executables.

604: /// * `false` if the file might be a binary
605: fn should_skip_file(file_name: &str, strict: bool) -> bool {
606:     // Skip hidden files

608:         return true;

616:     // In strict mode, also skip LICENSE/README files

620:             return true;
621:         }
622:     }
623: 
624:     false
625: }

860: impl TarFormat {
861:     pub fn from_file_name(filename: &str) -> Self {
862:         let filename = filename.to_lowercase();
863: 
864:         if let Some(idx) = filename.rfind(".tar.") {
865:             let ext = &filename[idx + 1..];
866:             let fmt = Self::from_ext(ext);
867:             if fmt != TarFormat::Raw {
868:                 return fmt;
869:             }
870:         }
871: 
872:         if let Some(ext) = Path::new(&filename).extension().and_then(|s| s.to_str()) {
873:             Self::from_ext(ext)
874:         } else {
875:             TarFormat::Raw
876:         }
877:     }

879:     pub fn from_ext(ext: &str) -> Self {
880:         ext.to_lowercase().parse().unwrap_or(TarFormat::Raw)
881:     }

883:     pub fn is_archive(&self) -> bool {
884:         match self {
885:             TarFormat::TarGz
886:             | TarFormat::TarXz
887:             | TarFormat::TarBz2
888:             | TarFormat::TarZst
889:             | TarFormat::Tar
890:             | TarFormat::Zip
891:             | TarFormat::SevenZip => true,
892:             TarFormat::Gz | TarFormat::Xz | TarFormat::Bz2 | TarFormat::Zst | TarFormat::Raw => {
893:                 false
894:             }
895:         }

898:     pub fn extension(&self) -> Option<&'static str> {

900:             TarFormat::TarGz => Some("tar.gz"),
901:             TarFormat::Gz => Some("gz"),
902:             TarFormat::TarXz => Some("tar.xz"),
903:             TarFormat::Xz => Some("xz"),
904:             TarFormat::TarBz2 => Some("tar.bz2"),
905:             TarFormat::Bz2 => Some("bz2"),
906:             TarFormat::TarZst => Some("tar.zst"),
907:             TarFormat::Zst => Some("zst"),
908:             TarFormat::Tar => Some("tar"),
909:             TarFormat::Zip => Some("zip"),
910:             TarFormat::SevenZip => Some("7z"),

918:     pub strip_components: usize,

921:     pub preserve_mtime: bool,

924: impl<'a> TarOptions<'a> {
925:     pub fn new(format: TarFormat) -> Self {

927:             format,
928:             strip_components: 0,
929:             pr: None,
930:             preserve_mtime: true,

935: pub fn untar(archive: &Path, dest: &Path, opts: &TarOptions) -> Result<()> {

937:         return unzip(
938:             archive,

940:             &ZipOptions {
941:                 strip_components: opts.strip_components,
942:             },
943:         );
944:     } else if opts.format == TarFormat::SevenZip {
945:         #[cfg(windows)]

947:             archive,
948:             dest,
949:             &SevenZipOptions {
950:                 strip_components: opts.strip_components,
951:             },
952:         );
953:     }
954: 
955:     debug!("tar -xf {} -C {}", archive.display(), dest.display());
956:     if let Some(pr) = &opts.pr {
957:         pr.set_message(format!(
958:             "extract {}",
959:             archive.file_name().unwrap().to_string_lossy()
960:         ));

964:         let archive = display_path(archive);

969:     let format = opts.format;

982: 
983:         if let Some(parent) = out_path.parent() {
984:             create_dir_all(parent).wrap_err_with(err)?;
985:         }
986:         let mut out = File::create(&out_path).wrap_err_with(err)?;
987:         std::io::copy(&mut reader, &mut out).wrap_err_with(err)?;
988:         return Ok(());
989:     }
990: 
991:     let tar = open_tar(format, archive)?;
992:     // TODO: put this back in when we can read+write in parallel
993:     // let mut cur = Cursor::new(vec![]);
994:     // let mut total = 0;
995:     // loop {
996:     //     let mut buf = Cursor::new(vec![0; 1024 * 1024]);
997:     //     let n = tar.read(buf.get_mut()).wrap_err_with(err)?;
998:     //     cur.get_mut().extend_from_slice(&buf.get_ref()[..n]);
999:     //     if n == 0 {
1000:     //         break;
1001:     //     }
1002:     //     if let Some(pr) = &opts.pr {
1003:     //         total += n as u64;
1004:     //         pr.set_length(total);
1005:     //     }
1006:     // }
1007:     create_dir_all(dest).wrap_err_with(err)?;
1008: 
1009:     // Try to extract using the tar crate, detecting sparse files during extraction
1010:     let mut needs_system_tar = false;
1011:     for entry in Archive::new(tar).entries().wrap_err_with(err)? {
1012:         let mut entry = entry.wrap_err_with(err)?;
1013: 
1014:         // Check if this is a GNU sparse file
1015:         if entry.header().entry_type().is_gnu_sparse() {
1016:             debug!("Detected GNU sparse file, falling back to system tar");
1017:             needs_system_tar = true;
1018:             // Clean up any partial extraction
1019:             remove_all(dest)?;
1020:             create_dir_all(dest)?;
1021:             break;
1022:         }
1023: 
1024:         // Configure mtime preservation based on options
1025:         entry.set_preserve_mtime(opts.preserve_mtime);
1026: 
1027:         trace!("extracting {}", entry.path().wrap_err_with(err)?.display());
1028:         entry.unpack_in(dest).wrap_err_with(err)?;
1029:     }
1030: 
1031:     // Check for the GNUSparseFile.0 directory which indicates the tar crate
1032:     // incorrectly handled a sparse file
1033:     if !needs_system_tar {
1034:         let sparse_dir = dest.join("GNUSparseFile.0");
1035:         if sparse_dir.exists() && sparse_dir.is_dir() {
1036:             debug!("Found GNUSparseFile.0 directory, using system tar");
1037:             needs_system_tar = true;
1038:             // Clean up the bad extraction
1039:             remove_all(dest)?;
1040:             create_dir_all(dest)?;
1041:         }
1042:     }
1043: 
1044:     if needs_system_tar {
1045:         // Use system tar for archives with problematic sparse files
1046:         // The tar crate doesn't properly handle certain GNU sparse formats
1047:         debug!("Using system tar for: {}", archive.display());
1048: 
1049:         // When preserve_mtime is false, use -m flag to not restore modification times
1050:         // This causes extracted files to have current time, which is important for
1051:         // cache invalidation and autopruning. Works on both BSD and GNU tar.
1052:         if !opts.preserve_mtime {
1053:             cmd!("tar", "-mxf", archive, "-C", dest)
1054:                 .run()
1055:                 .wrap_err_with(|| {
1056:                     format!("Failed to extract {} using system tar", archive.display())
1057:                 })?;
1058:         } else {
1059:             cmd!("tar", "-xf", archive, "-C", dest)
1060:                 .run()
1061:                 .wrap_err_with(|| {
1062:                     format!("Failed to extract {} using system tar", archive.display())
1063:                 })?;
1064:         }
1065:     }
1066: 
1067:     // Always use our manual strip to ensure consistent behavior across backends
1068:     strip_archive_path_components(dest, opts.strip_components).wrap_err_with(err)?;
1069:     Ok(())
1070: }

1072: fn open_tar(format: TarFormat, archive: &Path) -> Result<Box<dyn std::io::Read>> {
1073:     let f = File::open(archive)?;
1074:     Ok(match format {
1075:         // TODO: we probably shouldn't assume raw is tar.gz, but this was to retain existing behavior
1076:         TarFormat::TarGz | TarFormat::Gz | TarFormat::Raw => Box::new(GzDecoder::new(f)),
1077:         TarFormat::TarXz | TarFormat::Xz => Box::new(xz2::read::XzDecoder::new(f)),
1078:         TarFormat::TarBz2 | TarFormat::Bz2 => Box::new(BzDecoder::new(f)),
1079:         TarFormat::TarZst | TarFormat::Zst => Box::new(zstd::stream::read::Decoder::new(f)?),
1080:         TarFormat::Tar => Box::new(f),
1081:         TarFormat::Zip => bail!("zip format not supported"),
1082:         TarFormat::SevenZip => bail!("7z format not supported"),
1083:     })
1084: }
1085: 
1086: fn strip_archive_path_components(dir: &Path, strip_depth: usize) -> Result<()> {
1087:     if strip_depth == 0 {
1088:         return Ok(());
1089:     }
1090:     if strip_depth > 1 {
1091:         bail!("strip-components > 1 is not supported");
1092:     }
1093: 
1094:     let top_level_paths = ls(dir)?;
1095: 
1096:     for path in top_level_paths {
1097:         if !path.symlink_metadata()?.is_dir() {
1098:             continue;
1099:         }
1100: 
1101:         // rename the directory to a temp name to avoid conflicts when moving files
1102:         let temp_path = path.with_file_name(format!(
1103:             "{}_tmp_strip",
1104:             path.file_name().unwrap().to_string_lossy()
1105:         ));
1106:         fs::rename(&path, &temp_path)?;
1107: 
1108:         for entry in ls(&temp_path)? {
1109:             if let Some(file_name) = entry.file_name() {
1110:                 let dest_path = dir.join(file_name);
1111:                 fs::rename(entry, dest_path)?;
1112:             } else {
1113:                 continue;
1114:             }
1115:         }
1116: 
1117:         remove_dir(temp_path)?;
1118:     }
1119:     Ok(())
1120: }

1196:     let (file_name_base, ext) = file_name
1197:         .split_once('.')

1202: pub fn same_file(a: &Path, b: &Path) -> bool {
1203:     desymlink_path(a) == desymlink_path(b)
1204: }
1205: 
1206: pub fn desymlink_path(p: &Path) -> PathBuf {
1207:     if p.is_symlink()
1208:         && let Ok(target) = fs::read_link(p)
1209:     {
1210:         return target
1211:             .canonicalize()
1212:             .unwrap_or_else(|_| target.to_path_buf());
1213:     }
1214:     p.canonicalize().unwrap_or_else(|_| p.to_path_buf())
1215: }

1217: pub fn clone_dir(from: &PathBuf, to: &PathBuf) -> Result<()> {
1218:     if cfg!(macos) {
1219:         cmd!("/bin/cp", "-cR", from, to).run()?;
1220:     } else if cfg!(windows) {
1221:         cmd!("robocopy", from, to, "/MIR").run()?;
1222:     } else {
1223:         cmd!("cp", "--reflink=auto", "-r", from, to).run()?;
1224:     }
1225:     Ok(())
1226: }

1228: /// Inspects the top-level contents of a tar archive without extracting it
1229: /// Skips leading CurDir (".") components from a path's components iterator.
1230: /// Archives often have paths like "./foo/bar" where the leading "." should be ignored.
1231: fn skip_curdir_components(path: &Path) -> impl Iterator<Item = std::path::Component<'_>> {
1232:     path.components()
1233:         .skip_while(|c| matches!(c, std::path::Component::CurDir))
1234: }
1235: 
1236: pub fn inspect_tar_contents(archive: &Path, format: TarFormat) -> Result<Vec<(String, bool)>> {
1237:     let tar = open_tar(format, archive)?;
1238:     let mut archive = Archive::new(tar);
1239:     let mut top_level_components = std::collections::HashMap::new();
1240: 
1241:     for entry in archive.entries()? {
1242:         let entry = entry?;
1243:         let path = entry.path()?;
1244:         let header = entry.header();
1245: 
1246:         // Get the first non-CurDir component of the path (top-level directory/file)
1247:         let mut components = skip_curdir_components(&path);
1248: 
1249:         if let Some(first_component) = components.next() {
1250:             let name = first_component.as_os_str().to_string_lossy().to_string();
1251: 
1252:             // Check if this entry indicates the component is a directory
1253:             // It's a directory if the entry type is dir OR if there are more components after the first
1254:             let is_directory = header.entry_type().is_dir() || components.next().is_some();
1255: 
1256:             // Update the component's directory status
1257:             // A component is a directory if ANY entry indicates it's a directory
1258:             let existing = top_level_components.entry(name.clone()).or_insert(false);
1259:             *existing = *existing || is_directory;
1260:         }
1261:     }
1262: 
1263:     Ok(top_level_components.into_iter().collect())
1264: }
1265: 
1266: /// Inspects the top-level contents of a zip archive without extracting it
1267: pub fn inspect_zip_contents(archive: &Path) -> Result<Vec<(String, bool)>> {
1268:     let f = File::open(archive)?;
1269:     let mut archive = ZipArchive::new(f)
1270:         .wrap_err_with(|| format!("failed to open zip archive: {}", display_path(archive)))?;
1271:     let mut top_level_components = std::collections::HashMap::new();
1272: 
1273:     for i in 0..archive.len() {
1274:         let file = archive.by_index(i)?;
1275:         if let Some(path) = file.enclosed_name() {
1276:             // Get the first non-CurDir component of the path (top-level directory/file)
1277:             let mut components = skip_curdir_components(&path);
1278: 
1279:             if let Some(first_component) = components.next() {
1280:                 let name = first_component.as_os_str().to_string_lossy().to_string();
1281: 
1282:                 // Check if this entry indicates the component is a directory
1283:                 // It's a directory if the entry type is dir OR if there are more components after the first
1284:                 let is_directory = file.is_dir() || components.next().is_some();
1285: 
1286:                 let existing = top_level_components.entry(name.clone()).or_insert(false);
1287:                 *existing = *existing || is_directory;
1288:             }
1289:         }
1290:     }
1291: 
1292:     Ok(top_level_components.into_iter().collect())
1293: }
1294: 
1295: /// Adapted from inspect_tar_contents for 7z archives
1296: #[cfg(windows)]
1297: pub fn inspect_7z_contents(archive: &Path) -> Result<Vec<(String, bool)>> {
1298:     let sevenz = sevenz_rust::SevenZReader::open(archive, sevenz_rust::Password::empty())?;
1299:     let mut top_level_components = std::collections::HashMap::new();
1300: 
1301:     for file in &sevenz.archive().files {
1302:         let path = PathBuf::from(file.name());

1304:         // Get the first non-CurDir component of the path (top-level directory/file)
1305:         let mut components = skip_curdir_components(&path);
1306: 
1307:         if let Some(first_component) = components.next() {
1308:             let name = first_component.as_os_str().to_string_lossy().to_string();
1309:             // It's a directory if the entry type is dir OR if there are more components after the first
1310:             let is_directory = file.is_directory() || components.next().is_some();
1311: 
1312:             let existing = top_level_components.entry(name.clone()).or_insert(false);
1313:             *existing = *existing || is_directory;
1314:         }
1315:     }
1316: 
1317:     Ok(top_level_components.into_iter().collect())
1318: }

1322:     unimplemented!("7z format not supported on this platform")
1323: }
1324: 
1325: /// Determines if strip_components=1 should be applied based on archive structure
1326: pub fn should_strip_components(archive: &Path, format: TarFormat) -> Result<bool> {
1327:     let top_level_entries = match format {
1328:         TarFormat::Zip => inspect_zip_contents(archive)?,
1329:         TarFormat::SevenZip => inspect_7z_contents(archive)?,
1330:         _ => inspect_tar_contents(archive, format)?,
1331:     };
1332: 
1333:     // If there's exactly one top-level entry and it's a directory, we should strip it
1334:     if top_level_entries.len() == 1 {
1335:         let (_, is_directory) = &top_level_entries[0];
1336:         Ok(*is_directory)
1337:     } else {
1338:         Ok(false)
1339:     }
1340: }

Need understand aqua.rs run_github_attestation_check and maybe verify_github_artifact_attestations for similar pattern? Actually aqua uses attestation for the package. But the user is specifically about github backend archives. The phrase "Some GitHub releases attest each file inside an archive rather than the archive blob itself." This is a github backend scenario.

Need design a new internal verification path for "archive internal file attestation". Let's think about the best approach. We should modify `try_verify_github_attestations` to, on `NoAttestations` from the archive blob, try to extract the archive and verify each regular file inside. If all files verified, return true. If there are no files (unlikely), return NoAttestations. If any file fails verification, return Error. If extraction fails, return Error. We should also consider symlinks? Probably regular files only.

But we must ensure fail-closed: If the archive contains some files that are attested and some not, we must not accept. The safest is: every file in the archive must be attested (or at least every executable/file we care about). But what about metadata like README, LICENSE, etc. If the release attested each file inside, likely all files are attested, maybe including .txt? Need ask user about this. But maybe we can limit to "all non-directory entries" (including files). For empty directories no. But the user says "each file inside an archive" rather than archive blob, so it implies all files are attested individually. So to accept, we should require every file (regular file, not directory) inside to verify. If the archive contains a subdirectory that is itself an archive? Not typical.

Alternative: verify only files that are not in skip list? But fail-closed suggests we should not skip. But if a release only attests binaries and not docs, then accepting all files would fail. part.text: Now final summary to user. Include what changed, tests, commit hash. Keep concise.

Implemented and committed.

What changed in `src/backend/github.rs`:

- Added `verify_github_attestations_for_path`, a fail-closed verifier that first tries the existing archive-blob attestation path.
- If the blob has no attestation and the file is an archive, it extracts the archive and requires **every regular file inside** to be individually attested.
- Symlinks and other non-regular entries are rejected outright.
- Wired this into both lock-time (`verify_provenance_at_lock_time`) and install-time (`try_verify_github_attestations`) GitHub attestation verification.
- Preserved archive-level verification as the preferred path; the archive-internal path is only a fallback on `NoAttestations`.

Tests added in `src/backend/github.rs`:

- Blob-level attestation still wins and skips extraction.
- Non-archive files still return `NoAttestations`.
- Archive with all internal files attested succeeds.
- Missing attestation for any internal file fails closed.
- An internal file returning `Ok(false)` fails closed.
- Inner verification errors propagate.
- Empty archive fails closed.
- Nested files are verified.
- Zip archives are covered.
- Symlinks are rejected (Unix).

Verification:

- `cargo test --bins` → 944 passed.
- `cargo clippy --all-targets` → clean.
- `cargo fmt --check` → clean.
